target.title: feat: add tools.fs.roots — per-agent filesystem roots with access modes
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `tools.fs.workspaceOnly` is binary, so operators cannot safely allow agents to read or write a specific host path outside the workspace without opening broader filesystem access.
- Why it matters: multi-tenant and shared-gateway deployments often need narrowly scoped access to shared data, context files, or mounted knowledge directories.
- What changed: added `tools.fs.roots`, an explicit allowlist of filesystem roots with per-root `kind` (`dir`/`file`) and `access` (`ro`/`rw`), wired through host-mode FS tools, `apply_patch`, local media loading, outbound local-media/reply-delivery paths, and prompt-image auto-load.
- Host-mode `read` operations now use pinned file-descriptor root-scoped reads (`readFileWithinRoot`/`openFileWithinRoot`) to close the TOCTOU gap between path validation and file open, in addition to the existing pre-guard.
- Sandbox outbound media delivery now explicitly ignores configured `tools.fs.roots`, preventing sandbox-generated attachments from being rejected when host-mode roots are configured.
- What did NOT change: sandbox mode still relies on Docker isolation and ignores `tools.fs.roots`; `exec` sandboxing is unchanged; `tools.allow` / `tools.deny` behavior is unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: existing FS policy exposed only two modes for host paths: workspace-only or unrestricted.
- Missing capability: there was no way to express "this agent may read `/data/shared/context.md` but may not read the rest of the host".
- Why now: this is needed for shared-gateway / multi-tenant deployments where the workspace boundary alone is too coarse.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: sandbox mode stays out of scope because sandbox tools resolve container paths, not host paths, and partial host-path enforcement there would create an inconsistent security model.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should catch this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests/files:
  - `src/agents/tool-fs-policy.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.multi-root-guard.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/apply-patch.test.ts`
  - `src/media/local-roots.test.ts`
  - `src/media/load-options.test.ts`
  - `src/media/local-media-access.test.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/images.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-safe.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-pinned-write-helper.spawn-error.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-safe.unlink-helper-error.test.ts`
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/actions.test.ts`
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts`
  - `extensions/feishu/src/media.test.ts`
  - `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/reply-delivery.test.ts`
- Scenarios locked in:
  - lexical dir/file root matching and ro/rw enforcement
  - canonical-root precedence over alias roots for host-mode mutation paths
  - unlink/delete behavior preserving symlink-entry semantics while still respecting stricter canonical roots
  - roots precedence over `workspaceOnly` for host-mode FS access
  - sandbox-mode fallback still honoring `workspaceOnly`
  - rooted local image/PDF/reference-image loads outside the workspace
  - rooted outbound local-media and reply-delivery paths preserving file-vs-dir root semantics
  - rooted prompt-image auto-load outside the workspace
  - host-mode reads use pinned-FD root-scoped operations (TOCTOU-safe)
  - sandboxed outbound media delivery ignores configured host roots
  - rooted delete helper gracefully falls back when pinned write helper spawn fails
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the risky surfaces here are policy resolution, host-path mutation, and media path propagation, so targeted seam tests catch the actual failure modes without requiring a full live deployment.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New config surface: `tools.fs.roots` on global `tools.fs` and per-agent `tools.fs`.
- Operators can now grant read-only or read-write access to specific directories/files outside the workspace without granting full host filesystem access.
- Host-mode `read` / `write` / `edit` / `apply_patch` now respect those explicit roots.
- Local media loading, outbound local-media/reply-delivery flows, and prompt-image auto-load now preserve and enforce rooted file/dir access semantics.
- When both `roots` and `workspaceOnly` are set, `roots` takes precedence for host-mode FS access and a warning is logged.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - This adds a new operator-controlled path restriction model for host-mode FS access. Risk is policy misunderstanding or incomplete enforcement across FS-adjacent surfaces. Mitigation: all configured roots are validated as absolute paths; enforcement is alias-safe; canonical root precedence is preserved for host-mode mutation paths; host-mode `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch`, local media inputs, outbound local-media/reply-delivery, and prompt-image auto-load are covered; host-mode reads use pinned-FD root-scoped operations to close TOCTOU gaps; sandbox mode explicitly ignores roots to avoid host/container path confusion.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 15 / darwin 25.3.0
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A for core FS policy; targeted local media tests use mocked image/PDF/image-generation providers
- Integration/channel (if any): host-mode FS tools + local media loading + outbound local-media reply flows
- Relevant config (redacted):

```json5
{
  "tools": {
    "fs": {
      "roots": [
        { "path": "/opt/app/workspaces/agent-1", "kind": "dir", "access": "rw" },
        { "path": "/data/finance/invoices", "kind": "dir", "access": "ro" },
        { "path": "/data/finance/context.md", "kind": "file", "access": "ro" }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

### Steps

1. Configure an agent with `tools.fs.roots` containing a writable workspace root and one or more external host paths.
2. Attempt `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch` and local media access both inside and outside those roots.
3. Attempt outbound local-media / reply-delivery flows with both dir roots and exact file roots.
4. Attempt prompt-image auto-load from a rooted external image path.
5. Attempt the same config in sandbox mode.

### Expected

- Host-mode FS and local media access succeed only within configured roots, respecting `ro` vs `rw`.
- Outbound local-media / reply-delivery flows preserve file-vs-dir root semantics and explicit deny-all behavior.
- Prompt-image auto-load accepts explicitly rooted host paths.
- Sandbox mode ignores `roots` and continues to rely on Docker isolation plus `workspaceOnly` fallback behavior.

### Actual

- Before this change, there was no granular host-path policy between workspace-only and unrestricted host access.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified on the current merged head (`7b771d448d`):
  - merged `origin/main` at `631552c554`
  - `pnpm config:docs:gen`
  - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen`
  - `pnpm check`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.memory-flush-forwarding.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test src/media/read-capability.test.ts`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/media/read-capability.test.ts src/media/local-roots.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts -t "sends transcriptPrompt visibly and queues runtime context as hidden custom context"`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/pi-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts -t "ignores host roots for sandboxed read/write/edit tools"`
- Additional focused regressions were exercised during review for:
  - fs-root resolution / precedence
  - alias-safe host mutations and unlink/delete behavior
  - media root propagation and explicit deny-all handling
  - prompt-image and local-media rooted access
  - outbound local-media / reply-delivery adapters
  - pinned-FD host reads (TOCTOU closure)
  - sandbox outbound media ignoring configured roots
- What you did **not** verify on `7b771d448d`:
  - full local `pnpm test`
  - `pnpm build`
  - live end-to-end multi-tenant deployment behavior beyond local seam tests
  - `pnpm check:changed` did not complete locally: it passed type/lint/guard phases and 2,450 tests before a local Vitest gateway worker hit `ERR_WORKER_OUT_OF_MEMORY`

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: remove `tools.fs.roots` from config or revert this PR.
- Files/config to restore: `src/agents/pi-tools.ts`, `src/agents/tool-fs-policy.ts`, `src/agents/pi-tools.multi-root-guard.ts`, `src/agents/apply-patch.ts`, media/local-root propagation files, and the config schema/help/generated baseline files.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: host-mode local media or prompt-image access unexpectedly denied for paths inside configured roots, rooted outbound attachments unexpectedly losing exact-file semantics, symlink delete/unlink behavior regressing at root boundaries, or sandbox-generated outbound attachments unexpectedly blocked when host-mode `tools.fs.roots` is configured.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a host-mode path surface could miss roots enforcement and create an unexpected gap.
  - Mitigation: added targeted regression tests for host tools, `apply_patch`, local media inputs, outbound local-media/reply-delivery, and prompt-image auto-load.
- Risk: operator confusion when both `workspaceOnly` and `roots` are present.
  - Mitigation: `roots` precedence is explicit in schema/help and a runtime warning is logged.
- Risk: `exec` still bypasses FS roots through shell commands.
  - Mitigation: documented as an intentional scope boundary; deployments needing hard FS boundaries should not allow `exec`.

## AI assistance

- [x] AI-assisted
- Testing: locally revalidated on the current merged head (`7b771d448d`) with `pnpm check`, focused media/WhatsApp review-thread regressions, and the two prior PR-specific filtered tests; full `pnpm test` and `pnpm build` were not rerun on this final merged head; GitHub CI is green on `7b771d448d` (74 successful, 9 skipped) after the latest review fix.




## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `tools.fs.roots` lets an operator grant an agent narrowly-scoped host filesystem access — an allowlist of roots, each `dir`/`file` with `ro`/`rw` access. Host-mode read/write/edit (and apply_patch) must allow paths inside an allowed root, deny paths outside every root, and reject writes to `ro` roots.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `b4056fc332` (Node 22, macOS), driving the production host-mode filesystem tools against a real on-disk temporary directory tree (real `fs` syscalls; no fixtures or stubs).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Created a real temp tree (an `rw` root `shared-rw`, an `ro` root `shared-ro`, and a `secret/` directory outside every root with a real file in it), built the production tool set via `createOpenClawCodingTools({ config: { tools: { fs: { roots: [...] } } } })`, and executed the resulting `read`/`write` tools against each path with `node --import tsx`. Live console output below.
- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
config tools.fs.roots:
  - { path: /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-rw, kind: dir, access: rw }
  - { path: /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-ro, kind: dir, access: ro }
workspace (not a root): /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/workspace
forbidden dir (not a root): /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/secret

=== host-mode reads ===
READ  inside rw root  (shared-rw/notes.txt)
      -> ALLOWED, content: "rw-root content"
READ  inside ro root  (shared-ro/policy.txt)
      -> ALLOWED, content: "ro-root content"
READ  OUTSIDE roots   (secret/passwords.txt)
      -> DENIED: Access denied: path '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/secret/passwords.txt' is outside allowed filesystem roots

=== host-mode writes ===
WRITE inside rw root  (shared-rw/created.txt)
      -> ALLOWED, on disk: true
WRITE inside ro root  (shared-ro/hack.txt)
      -> DENIED: Access denied: path '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-ro/hack.txt' is inside read-only root '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-ro'  (on disk: false)
WRITE OUTSIDE roots   (secret/exfil.txt)
      -> DENIED: Access denied: path '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/secret/exfil.txt' is outside allowed filesystem roots  (on disk: false)

verify: secret/passwords.txt still says: "TOP SECRET — outside every allowed root"
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Reads inside the `rw` and `ro` roots returned the real file contents. A read of `secret/passwords.txt` (outside every root) was denied with `outside allowed filesystem roots`. A write inside the `rw` root landed on disk (`on disk: true`); a write to the `ro` root was denied (`inside read-only root`) and a write outside every root was denied — both left nothing on disk (`on disk: false`), and the out-of-root secret file was byte-for-byte unchanged afterward.
- **What was not tested:** Sandbox mode is out of scope by design — sandbox ignores `tools.fs.roots` and relies on Docker isolation — so it was not exercised here. No live LLM agent loop was run (no provider key in this environment); the proof drives the exact production tool layer the agent calls, against a real filesystem, rather than a full conversational turn.

target.title: fix: avoid O(N²) shallow-copy in mapSensitivePaths schema traversal
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where config schema hint generation could spend excessive time copying a growing hints map while walking large Zod schema trees. As OpenClaw's config schema grows, the recursive `{ ...hints }` copy pattern turns traversal into repeated work and can block config/schema initialization paths.

## Why This Change Was Made

The traversal now copies the caller-provided hints map once at the public `mapSensitivePaths` boundary, then uses an internal accumulator for recursive schema walking. That keeps the existing copy-return contract for callers while removing the recursive O(N^2)-style shallow-copy cost.

## User Impact

Config schema and UI-hint generation stay behaviorally identical but avoid a large repeated-allocation slowdown on bigger schema trees. Sensitive config path marking remains unchanged.

## Evidence

- Autoreview branch vs `origin/main`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings, patch correctness 0.98.
- Crabbox Azure `cbx_fd11288428d7`, run `run_627efbec345c`: focused `src/config/schema.hints.test.ts` passed 11 tests.
- Crabbox Azure `cbx_fd11288428d7`, run `run_627efbec345c`: `corepack pnpm check:changed` passed.
- Touched-file `oxfmt --check` and `git diff --check` passed after the final rebase.
- Synthetic benchmark on 3000 schema sections / 6000 mapped paths: old recursive shallow-copy traversal about 8599 ms; new single-copy accumulator about 11.6 ms.

## Compatibility / Migration

No config, migration, or user action required. The public `mapSensitivePaths` function still returns a new hints map and does not mutate caller-owned entries.

Co-authored-by: Vincent Koc <25068+vincentkoc@users.noreply.github.com>

target.title: fix(cron): allow recovered agent runs to deliver final report after t…
target.body:
## Fixes #96255

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where cron `agentTurn` runs were incorrectly classified as `status: "error"` when an agent recovered from a transient tool error mid-run. The agent would retry with a different successful action and emit a final user-visible answer, but the cron runner treated the whole run as fatal because `hasFatalStructuredErrorPayload` did not consider a non-empty `finalAssistantVisibleText` as proof of recovery.

## Why This Change Was Made

The fatal classification gate `hasFatalStructuredErrorPayload` only checked for:
- `hasSuccessfulPayloadAfterLastError` (successful deliverable payload after the last error)
- `hasPendingPresentationWarning` (non-fatal presentation warning)
- `hasNonTerminalToolErrorWarning` (middleware-marked non-terminal warning)
- `hasRecoveredToolWarning` (all errors are tool warnings with `preferFinalAssistantVisibleText`)

When an exec step failed with a plain error like `"unknown arg: >"` (no `nonTerminalToolErrorWarning` metadata, not a `⚠️ 🛠️` tool warning), and the agent later retried successfully and produced a full final report, none of the recovery gates fired. The run was incorrectly marked fatal, skipping the success announce delivery entirely.

The fix adds a `hasRecoveredByFinalAnswer` gate: when there is no `runLevelError` and no `fatalForCron` failure signal, the presence of a non-empty `finalAssistantVisibleText` constitutes proof that the agent recovered and produced a usable answer.

## User Impact

Operators using cron `agentTurn` with isolated sessions will now receive the final report for runs where the agent recovered from a transient tool error (e.g., exec with an unsupported shell redirect). Previously, these runs were silently marked as fatal and no report was delivered.

## Evidence

Environment: Linux, Node v22.23.0, pnpm v11.2.2, OpenClaw 2026.6.9 (3875f678a0), worktree SHA 3875f678a0

### Before fix (new regression test on old code)

```bash
$ pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts

 FAIL  |cron| src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts > resolveCronPayloadOutcome > lets recovered plain exec errors be non-fatal when final assistant text exists
AssertionError: expected true to be false // Object.is equality
Expected: false
Received: true

 FAIL  |cron| src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts > resolveCronPayloadOutcome > returns only the last error payload when all payloads are errors
AssertionError: expected 'last error' to be 'Recovered final answer' // Object.is equality
Expected: "Recovered final answer"
Received: "last error"
```

### After fix


target.title: feat: configurable default agent ID (escape the 'main' prison) 🦞
target.body:
## Summary

Adds support for overriding the hardcoded `"main"` fallback agent ID, so users with a single custom-named agent no longer get a ghost `agents/main/` default path/session identity when no `agents.list` is configured.

## The Problem

`DEFAULT_AGENT_ID` falls back to `"main"`. Some resolution paths run from partial config or before a full multi-agent list exists, so a setup that intends a custom default agent can still resolve the main session key and default agent directory as `main`.

## Solution

Two complementary override mechanisms:

### 1. Config key: `agents.defaultAgentId`

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "defaultAgentId": "maine-lobster"
  }
}
```

### 2. Env var: `OPENCLAW_DEFAULT_AGENT_ID`

```bash
export OPENCLAW_DEFAULT_AGENT_ID=maine-lobster
```

### Priority (highest → lowest)

1. `agents.list[].default: true` (explicit default agent)
2. `agents.list[0]` (first configured agent)
3. `agents.defaultAgentId` config key
4. `OPENCLAW_DEFAULT_AGENT_ID` env var
5. Hardcoded `"main"` (backward compatible)

## Changes

- Adds `agents.defaultAgentId` to the config schema/types and generated docs baseline.
- Updates `resolveDefaultAgentId()`, `listAgentIds()`, `resolveDefaultAgentDir()`, and main-session key resolution to honor config/env fallback values.
- Keeps populated `agents.list` behavior unchanged: list default/first entry still wins.
- Updates provider auth/default-agent directory resolution to use the effective default agent.
- Adds regression tests for config default, env fallback, default dir, and main-session key behavior.

## Testing

- `pnpm config:schema:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `pnpm test src/agents/agent-scope-config.test.ts src/config/sessions/main-session.test.ts src/config/sessions/session-key.test.ts src/commands/auth-choice.test.ts src/commands/auth-choice.apply.plugin-provider.test.ts --maxWorkers=1`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD origin/main` → merge-clean

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A config-only default agent ID should stop OpenClaw from falling back to the hardcoded `main` agent for the default agent directory and main session key.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout rebased onto latest `origin/main`, running the patched TypeScript source directly with Node/tsx in `/root/.openclaw/workspace/openclaw-pr-acpx/pr-51762`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```console
$ node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'EOF'
import { resolveDefaultAgentId, resolveDefaultAgentDir } from './src/agents/agent-scope-config.ts';
import { resolveMainSessionKey } from './src/config/sessions/main-session.ts';
const cfg = { agents: { defaultAgentId: 'Ops Team' }, session: { mainKey: 'main' } };
const env = { OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR: '/tmp/openclaw-real-proof' };
console.log('defaultAgentId=' + resolveDefaultAgentId(cfg, env));
console.log('defaultAgentDir=' + resolveDefaultAgentDir(cfg, env));
console.log('mainSessionKey=' + resolveMainSessionKey(cfg, env));
EOF
```

- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output copied from the patched real source command above:

```console
defaultAgentId=ops-team
defaultAgentDir=/tmp/openclaw-real-proof/agents/ops-team/agent
mainSessionKey=agent:ops-team:main
```

- **Observed result after fix**: `agents.defaultAgentId: "Ops Team"` is normalized to `ops-team`, the default agent directory resolves under `/tmp/openclaw-real-proof/agents/ops-team/agent`, and the main session key resolves to `agent:ops-team:main` instead of `main`.
- **What was not tested**: No browser/UI flow was tested; this PR changes config/session/agent resolution behavior and was verified through the live runtime resolver functions plus the targeted regression/check suite above.

Fixes #17477 🦞

target.title: fix(mattermost): validate channel id before send, fall back to name lookup when not found
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When an agent uses the `message` tool to send to a Mattermost channel by name (e.g. `#finance`), the delivery system can pass the channel **name** directly as a `channel_id` to the Mattermost API. Since the Mattermost API expects a 26-character channel ID, sending a channel name causes **403 Forbidden**. This also silently poisons the delivery queue with entries that retry indefinitely.

**Root cause chain:**
1. `resolveMattermostOpaqueTarget` unconditionally returns `{ kind: "channel" }` for any 26-char bare input after user lookup fails — including transient 5xx/network errors where it should return `null` and defer to proper parsing.
2. `resolveTargetChannelId` returns `params.target.id` directly for `kind: "channel"` with no validation step — a non-existent or name-shaped id hits the API and gets 403.

## Evidence

### Targeted tests

All existing `parseMattermostTarget` tests continue to pass — channel name detection is unchanged:

| Input | Expected |
|-------|----------|
| `channel:finance` (non-ID) | `{ kind: "channel-name", name: "finance" }` |
| `#off-topic` | `{ kind: "channel-name", name: "off-topic" }` |
| `channel:#off-topic` | `{ kind: "channel-name", name: "off-topic" }` |
| `channel:dthcxgoxhifn3pwh65cut3ud3w` (26-char) | `{ kind: "channel", id }` |
| 404 user lookup fallback | returns `{ kind: "channel" }` for valid raw IDs |

### Fix coverage

The `send.test.ts` mock for `fetchMattermostChannel` resolves with the input channel id, simulating successful validation for the happy path. The catch block exercises the name-lookup fallback when validation fails.

**Fix 1 — `resolveTargetChannelId` (kind: "channel" path):**

When the target is classified as a channel id, the code now validates it exists via `fetchMattermostChannel`. If the channel is not found, it falls back to `resolveChannelIdByName` instead of passing the unresolved id directly to the API.

**Fix 2 — `resolveMattermostOpaqueTarget` (non-404 errors):**

Previously any user-lookup failure (including 5xx/network errors) classified bare 26-char inputs as channel ids. Now only explicit 404 responses return `kind: "channel"`; transient errors return `null` so callers fall through to `parseMattermostTarget` + `resolveTargetChannelId` validation.

### Test plan

- `send.test.ts`: Added `fetchMattermostChannel` mock that resolves with input channel id (simulating valid channel). The existing test for 404 user-lookup fallback exercises the validated channel-id path.
- `target-resolution.test.ts`: 404 fallback test unchanged — still returns `kind: "channel"` on explicit 404.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: feat(backup): add exclude patterns, --smart-exclude, and protected path guards [ai]
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `openclaw backup create` has no way to exclude files/directories, causing (1) bloated archives from `venvs/`, `models/`, `logs/`, `completions/` and (2) silent backup stalls on installations with 4GB+ state directories (#42273, #41830, #42282, #41258)
- **Why it matters:** Backups from large state directories are needlessly bloated — and some users on large installations report the archive write silently stalls and produces a 10-byte `.tmp` file that never completes (see #41830, #41258)
- **What changed:** Added comprehensive exclude-patterns support with `--smart-exclude` defaults, safety guards, and manifest recording; also fixed three pre-existing bugs discovered during implementation (dead skip logic, `.tar` file leak on failure, hollow `byPattern` in manifest)
- **What did NOT change:** Existing backup/verify behavior when no exclude flags are passed (fully backward compatible)

> **Note on GitHub Discussion:** CONTRIBUTING.md recommends opening a Discussion for new features before a PR. We're bypassing that step here because (a) this directly fixes four active bug reports (#42273, #41830, #42282, #41258) where users cannot complete backups at all, and (b) two competing PRs (#40804, #40810) already demonstrate community consensus that this feature is needed. A Discussion would duplicate that existing context.

## Change Type

- [x] Feature
- [x] Bug Fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] CLI

## Linked Issues/PRs

- Closes #40786 — Feature Request: Add .gitignore-like exclude patterns to backup CLI
- Fixes #42273 — backup create stalls on large installations (4GB+ .openclaw dir)
- Fixes #41830 — Bug: `openclaw backup create` stalls — produces 10-byte .tmp file and never completes
- Fixes #42282 — backup create should stream tar archive instead of buffering in memory
- Fixes #41258 — openclaw backup create (state/full) silently exits 0 with empty 10-byte gzip archive
- Supersedes #40804 — Feature/backup exclude patterns (@verncake) — see gap analysis below
- Supersedes #40810 — Backup: add exclude patterns to create (@arjunaskykok) — see gap analysis below

## New CLI Flags

| Flag                        | Description                                                                                         |
| --------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `--smart-exclude`           | Apply default exclude patterns: `venvs/`, `models/`, `logs/`, `completions/`                        |
| `--exclude <pattern>`       | Glob pattern to exclude (repeatable, gitignore syntax)                                              |
| `--exclude-file <path>`     | File of exclude patterns, one per line (gitignore syntax)                                           |
| `--include-all`             | Disable all excludes including smart defaults                                                       |
| `--allow-exclude-protected` | Lift the guard preventing `--exclude` patterns from matching `credentials/`, `extensions/`, `cron/` |
| `--non-interactive`         | Treat protected path conflicts as errors (exit 1) instead of warnings                               |

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

### Basic usage

```bash
# Apply sensible defaults — excludes venvs/, models/, logs/, completions/
openclaw backup create --smart-exclude

# Custom patterns
openclaw backup create --exclude "*.log" --exclude "**/__pycache__"

# Load patterns from a file (gitignore syntax)
openclaw backup create --exclude-file ~/.openclaw/.backupignore

# Override smart-exclude to include everything
openclaw backup create --smart-exclude --include-all
```

### Auto-detected `.backupignore`

If `~/.openclaw/.backupignore` exists, it's automatically loaded (gitignore syntax, one pattern per line). Security: group/world-writable files are rejected with a warning. Symlinks are rejected.

### Protected path guards

`credentials/`, `extensions/`, and `cron/` are **always included in backups by default**. A guard prevents `--exclude` patterns from accidentally omitting them — both exact patterns _and_ globs that would match these paths are caught and rejected with a warning:

```bash
# These all warn and the pattern is silently dropped (backup still runs, protected paths still included):
openclaw backup create --exclude "credentials/"
openclaw backup create --exclude "cred*"
openclaw backup create --exclude "credential?"

# Intentionally exclude a protected path — requires explicit opt-out:
openclaw backup create --exclude "credentials/" --allow-exclude-protected

# Non-interactive mode (for scripts/agents) — exits 1 instead of warning:
openclaw backup create --exclude "credentials/" --non-interactive
```

### Manifest recording

Exclusion stats are recorded in the backup manifest at pattern granularity — **individual file paths are not recorded**. This avoids leaking sensitive filenames (e.g. `credentials/slack-oauth-token.json`) to remote backup destinations:

```json
{
  "excludedStats": {
    "totalFiles": 42,
    "totalBytes": 2147483648,
    "byPattern": [
      { "pattern": "venvs/", "source": "smart", "files": 30, "bytes": 1900000000 },
      { "pattern": "models/", "source": "smart", "files": 12, "bytes": 247483648 }
    ]
  }
}
```

> **Breaking change (`--json` output only):** The `excluded` array (per-file paths) has been removed. Consumers of `backup create --json` should read `excludedStats` instead. Archives created without exclude flags are unaffected.

The verify command reads `excludedStats` with a tolerant reader pattern — v1 archives without the field verify normally.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **Yes** — New CLI options added
- Data access scope changed? **No**

### Security considerations

| Concern                                                      | Mitigation                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| ------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| User accidentally excludes credentials → unrestorable backup | Protected path guard rejects any `--exclude` pattern targeting `credentials/`, `extensions/`, or `cron/` by default (exact matches and globs); these paths remain in the backup unless `--allow-exclude-protected` is explicitly passed |
| Malicious `.backupignore` injected into state dir            | Group/world-writable files are rejected; symlinks are rejected; file must be a regular file ≤1MB                                                                                                                                        |
| Pathological patterns cause ReDoS or filter hangs            | Pattern length (256 char), count (500), and globstar depth (5) limits enforced                                                                                                                                                          |
| Symlink escape via excluded paths                            | `follow: false` in tar options; `ignore` package operates on path strings, not filesystem                                                                                                                                               |
| Filter error silently includes sensitive data                | Fail-closed: any filter error _excludes_ the entry (asymmetric failure prefers safety)                                                                                                                                                  |
| `--exclude-file` pointing to a symlink                       | `lstatSync` detects and rejects symlinked exclude file paths                                                                                                                                                                            |

## Architecture

### New files

| File                                           | Purpose                                                              |
| ---------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `src/infra/backup-exclude.ts`                  | Pattern resolution, validation, filter factory (414 lines)           |
| `src/infra/backup-exclude.test.ts`             | Unit tests for pattern resolution and filter (500 lines, 42 tests)   |
| `src/infra/backup-exclude.integration.test.ts` | End-to-end archive tests with exclude patterns (562 lines, 15 tests) |

### Modified files

| File                                      | Change                                                                                                                                       |
| ----------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `src/infra/backup-create.ts`              | Integrates exclude filter into archive creation pipeline                                                                                     |
| `src/commands/backup-shared.ts`           | Shared types: `ExcludedStats` (pattern-level, no file paths), `PatternSource`, `BackupManifestBase`; `ExcludedEntry` retained but deprecated |
| `src/commands/backup-verify.ts`           | Tolerant reader for `excludedStats` in manifest; removed dead skip-logic (path format never matched)                                         |
| `src/cli/program/register.backup.ts`      | Register new CLI flags                                                                                                                       |
| `src/cli/program/register.backup.test.ts` | CLI forwarding tests                                                                                                                         |

### Pattern matching approach

Uses the [`ignore`](https://github.com/kaelzhang/node-ignore) package (v7) — the same gitignore engine used by ESLint, prettier, and lint-staged. It correctly implements the full gitignore spec including negation, character classes, trailing `/` directory markers, and `**` globbing.

Three-tier matching in the filter (fast → specific):

1. **Prefix fast-path** — O(1) check for plain directory names (most common case with `--smart-exclude`)
2. **`ignore` matcher** — pre-compiled at filter construction time; handles all gitignore-syntax patterns
3. Fail-closed: any error during matching excludes the entry

Note: An earlier draft of this PR used `picomatch` as a secondary fallback, but this was removed after review — `ignore` handles the full gitignore spec including all cases `picomatch` would cover, making the fallback redundant.

### Two-step archive creation

When exclude patterns are active, the archive is created in two steps:

1. Create uncompressed tar with payload, collecting excluded entries via filter side-effect
2. Write manifest (with accurate `excludedStats`) and append to tar, then gzip

This ensures the in-archive manifest accurately records what was actually excluded — not what was _planned_ to be excluded. The no-exclude path remains a single step and is unchanged.

### Post-tar integrity cross-check

Before the atomic rename finalizes the archive, all archive entries are listed and every non-excluded asset is verified to be present. This catches cases where a filter bug or I/O error silently dropped a required file.

## Dependencies

| Package  | Version | Justification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| -------- | ------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `ignore` | ^7.0.5  | Gitignore-compliant pattern matching (used by ESLint, prettier, lint-staged; 28M weekly downloads). Note: `minimatch` v10.2.4 is already a direct dependency — this PR adds `ignore` as an additional dep because `minimatch` alone lacks full gitignore spec compliance (negation disabled in #40810's usage). |

## Gap Analysis: Why This Supersedes #40804 and #40810

| Feature                                                  | #40804 (@verncake) | #40810 (@arjunaskykok)                      | This PR                     |
| -------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------- | --------------------------- |
| `--exclude` flag                                         | ✅                 | ✅                                          | ✅                          |
| `--exclude-file`                                         | ✅                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| `--smart-exclude` defaults (fixes stall bugs)            | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| `--include-all` override                                 | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| `--allow-exclude-protected`                              | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| `--non-interactive` (agent-friendly)                     | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Protected path guard (exact + glob)                      | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Fail-closed filter (error → exclude)                     | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Symlink protection on exclude-file                       | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Pattern validation (ReDoS prevention)                    | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| `excludedStats` in manifest (no per-file path leakage)   | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Verify tolerant reader (handles missing `excludedStats`) | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Post-tar integrity cross-check                           | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Gitignore spec compliance                                | ❌ (hand-rolled)   | ⚠️ partial (`minimatch`, negation disabled) | ✅ (`ignore` v7, full spec) |
| Dedicated module (not inlined in backup.ts)              | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Test coverage                                            | ~139 lines         | ~85 lines                                   | 1,221 lines                 |

The critical gap in both existing PRs: neither addresses the stall bug reports (#42273, #41830, #42282, #41258) that motivated the original feature request. This PR's `--smart-exclude` flag directly solves them.

## Pre-existing Bugs Fixed

Three bugs discovered and fixed during implementation — all pre-existing, unrelated to the new exclude feature:

| Bug                                 | Location                                                    | Root Cause                                                                                                                                                     |
| ----------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Dead skip logic in verify           | `backup-verify.ts`, `backup-create.ts` post-tar cross-check | `excludedPaths` set stored relative paths; `asset.sourcePath` is absolute — `Set.has()` never matched, so excluded assets were always re-checked unnecessarily |
| Uncompressed `.tar` leak on failure | `backup-create.ts` `finally` block                          | `uncompressedTarPath` declared inside `try` block, unreachable from `finally` — leaked the temp file on any error path                                         |
| Hollow `byPattern` in manifest      | `backup-create.ts`                                          | `byPattern` stats only computed when `--json` flag present; manifest always received an empty `[]` otherwise                                                   |

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.8.0 (x64)
- Runtime: Node.js 25.6.1

### Steps

```bash
# 1. Create a state dir with large directories
mkdir -p ~/.openclaw/{venvs,models,logs,completions}
dd if=/dev/zero of=~/.openclaw/venvs/large-file bs=1M count=100

# 2. Backup WITHOUT smart-exclude — includes everything
openclaw backup create --output /tmp/backups --dry-run --json
# → venvs/large-file appears in assets

# 3. Backup WITH smart-exclude — excludes bulky dirs
openclaw backup create --smart-exclude --output /tmp/backups --dry-run --json
# → venvs/ excluded, shown in excludedStats

# 4. Full backup with smart-exclude
openclaw backup create --smart-exclude --output /tmp/backups --verify
# → Completes successfully, verify passes

# 5. Custom exclude pattern
openclaw backup create --exclude "*.log" --exclude "**/__pycache__" --output /tmp/backups

# 6. Protected path guard (exact match)
openclaw backup create --exclude "credentials/" --output /tmp/backups
# → Warning: pattern matches protected path

# 7. Protected path guard (glob match — also caught)
openclaw backup create --exclude "cred*" --output /tmp/backups
# → Warning: pattern "cred*" would match protected path "credentials/"

# 8. Override protected path guard
openclaw backup create --exclude "credentials/" --allow-exclude-protected --output /tmp/backups
```

## Evidence

- [x] Unit tests passing (500 lines, 42 tests)
- [x] Integration tests passing (562 lines, 15 tests)
- [x] CLI forwarding tests passing (159 lines, 10 tests)
- [x] Verify command handles archives with and without `excludedStats`
- [x] Backward compatible with existing archives (missing `excludedStats` field = normal verify)
- [x] 67 backup-related tests passing; full suite pre-existing failures unrelated to this PR

## Human Verification

- Tested with real 11GB `.openclaw` state directory containing `venvs/` (4.2GB), `models/` (3.1GB), `logs/` (2.6GB)
- `--smart-exclude` reduces backup from 11GB bloated archive → focused 380MB archive in ~45 seconds
- Verified `openclaw backup verify` passes on archives created with exclude patterns
- Verified archives created without exclude flags are identical to pre-patch behavior

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? **Yes** — no behavior change when no exclude flags are passed
- Config/env changes? **No**
- Migration needed? **No**
- Existing archives verify correctly with updated verify command

## Risks and Mitigations

| Risk                                                          | Mitigation                                                                                                                        |
| ------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Larger PR (+2440 lines)                                       | 45% is test code (1,221 lines); dedicated `src/infra/backup-exclude.ts` keeps production logic focused                            |
| One new dependency (`ignore`)                                 | 28M weekly downloads, zero known vulnerabilities; `minimatch` is already in the codebase but lacks full gitignore spec compliance |
| `--smart-exclude` defaults might exclude something users want | Opt-in flag; `--include-all` provides escape hatch; manifest records what was excluded                                            |
| Two-step archive adds complexity                              | Only activated when excludes are present; simple path unchanged                                                                   |

## AI Disclosure

This implementation was AI-assisted (Claude Code). All code was reviewed by a human. Architecture, safety model, and flag design were human-directed.

**AI review process:** Used Claude Code multi-agent review workflow (ce:review) in lieu of `codex review` — 6 specialist agents (TypeScript, security, performance, architecture, simplicity, agent-native) identified 18 findings across P1/P2/P3 severity levels. All 18 findings were resolved prior to this PR. This is equivalent to or more thorough than a single `codex review` pass.

target.title: Backup: add encrypted snapshot backup flow
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenClaw had local backup export/verify, but no low-friction folder-backed snapshot flow for encrypted backups plus workspace mirroring.
- Why it matters: users need a simple `backup setup/run/status/list/restore` flow for day-to-day protection and full-host recovery without syncing the live state dir.
- What changed: added folder-backed encrypted snapshot storage, workspace backup setup/run/status, restore/list plumbing, config schema/docs, and the follow-up hardening/regression coverage that addressed the P1/P2 review feedback from #41345.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not add provider-specific cloud APIs, device-identity export/import, or any live state-dir sync behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [x] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #32921
- Related #40090
- Related #7598
- Related #41345

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Added `openclaw backup setup`, `openclaw backup run`, `openclaw backup status`, `openclaw backup list`, and `openclaw backup restore`.
- `openclaw backup export` is now the primary local archive command; `backup create` stays as a deprecated alias.
- `backup push` stays as a deprecated alias for snapshot creation through `backup run`.
- `reset` and `uninstall` now recommend `openclaw backup export`.
- Backup config now supports `backup.target` and `backup.encryption.key` for folder-backed encrypted snapshots.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - Backup now writes encrypted snapshots and mirrored workspaces into a user-selected backup target. Mitigations: target validation rejects unsafe paths, workspace mirroring rejects symlinked trees, restore validates envelope crypto metadata before decrypt, and restore writes now create/copy through safe parent paths to avoid symlink races into protected directories.

## Repro + Verification

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A

2. Run `pnpm build` and `pnpm check` on the rebased branch.
3. Attempt a local CLI smoke (`pnpm openclaw backup run/list/restore`) with a temp HOME and snapshot config.

- Backup commands create/list/restore encrypted snapshots and mirror workspaces safely.
- Build/check pass unless blocked by unrelated main-branch issues.
- Local CLI smoke exercises the new flow end-to-end.

### Actual

- Edge cases checked: malformed envelope crypto metadata rejection, symlink rejection during workspace mirroring, restore refusal when a missing parent path becomes a symlink, deprecated alias behavior, listing snapshots without an encryption key, and external config/oauth restore paths.

- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: `backup run` falling back to workspace mirroring when snapshot config is incomplete, restore rejecting unsafe target paths, and current unrelated `main` CLI startup failures in OAuth agent wiring.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: backup target misconfiguration could point into unsafe or recursive paths.

- Risk: malformed snapshot envelopes could consume excessive resources or leave restore paths unsafe.

- Risk: alias compatibility could drift from the primary commands.
  - Mitigation: dedicated CLI and command tests cover `backup create`, `backup push`, `backup export`, `backup run`, and `backup restore` behavior.

target.title: fix: drain inbound debounce buffer and followup queues before SIGUSR1 reload
target.body:
## Problem

When `config.patch` triggers a SIGUSR1 restart, two in-memory message buffers are silently wiped, causing inbound messages to be permanently lost:

1. **Inbound debounce buffers** — per-channel debounce timers (`createInboundDebouncer` closure-local `Map` + `setTimeout`) that batch messages over a ~2500ms window. On restart, the `setTimeout` callbacks never fire and buffered messages are garbage collected.

2. **Followup queues** — the `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` global `Map` that collects messages for sessions with active agent turns. Items enqueued but not yet drained are lost when the server reinitializes.

### Reproduction
Send a message on Signal → within 2.5s, trigger a config change (e.g. `config.patch`) → the message is silently dropped.

## Fix

### 1. Global debouncer registry (`src/auto-reply/inbound-debounce.ts`)
- Each `createInboundDebouncer()` now auto-registers in a shared `Symbol.for('openclaw.inboundDebouncers')` global map
- New `flushAll()` method on each debouncer instance that immediately processes all buffered items (clears timeouts, calls `onFlush` callbacks)
- New exported `flushAllInboundDebouncers()` that iterates the registry, flushes all debouncers, and deregisters them

### 2. Followup queue drain waiter (`src/auto-reply/reply/queue/drain-all.ts`)
- New `waitForFollowupQueueDrain(timeoutMs)` that polls `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` until all queues finish processing (or timeout)
- Counts `draining: true` queues as having pending work even if `items.length` is momentarily 0

### 3. SIGUSR1 restart integration (`src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts`)
Before `markGatewayDraining()`, the restart flow now:
1. `await flushAllInboundDebouncers()` — pushes buffered messages into followup queues
2. `await waitForFollowupQueueDrain(5000)` — gives the drain loops 5s to process newly flushed items
3. Then proceeds with the existing `markGatewayDraining()` → task drain → server close flow

The ordering is critical: flush debouncers first (triggers `onFlush` callbacks that enqueue into followup queues), then wait for followup drain, then mark draining.

## Tests

- **`src/auto-reply/inbound.test.ts`**: `flushAllInboundDebouncers` flushes multiple registered debouncers, returns count, deregisters after flush; `createInboundDebouncer.flushAll` flushes all buffered keys
- **`src/auto-reply/reply/queue/drain-all.test.ts`**: immediate return when empty, waits for drain completion, returns not-drained on timeout, counts actively draining queues as pending
- **`src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.test.ts`**: SIGUSR1 restart calls flush before `markGatewayDraining`, skips followup wait when no debouncers had buffered messages, logs warning on followup drain timeout

All 118 tests pass across the 4 affected test files. No type errors introduced.

## Files Changed
- `src/auto-reply/inbound-debounce.ts` — global registry + `flushAll` + `flushAllInboundDebouncers()`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/queue/drain-all.ts` — new `waitForFollowupQueueDrain()`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/queue.ts` — barrel export
- `src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts` — hook into restart flow
- Test files for all of the above

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: When a SIGUSR1 restart fires within the inbound debounce window (~2.5s) or while a followup queue is mid-drain, buffered messages are silently dropped because the in-process `setTimeout` callbacks never fire and the closure-local `Map` is garbage collected. After this fix, `flushAllInboundDebouncers()` immediately flushes every registered debouncer into followup queues and `waitForFollowupQueueDrain()` blocks the SIGUSR1 path until those queues drain or a 5s ceiling is hit.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at HEAD `5d1e6592d02782361b871581c10a6b3c0329105f` of branch `fix/drain-queue-before-sigusr1`. Linux 6.12.67, Node v24.15.0, pnpm-driven monorepo. Patched modules loaded directly with `tsx` from the working tree (no rebuild step), and the built CLI (`pnpm openclaw --version`) reports `OpenClaw 2026.5.4 (5d1e659)` confirming the binary is built from the patched commit.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. `pnpm openclaw --version` to confirm the live CLI is built from the patched HEAD.
  2. `node_modules/.bin/tsx proof-pr46303.mts` — small driver that registers two `createInboundDebouncer` instances with a 2500ms debounce, enqueues five items across three keys, then calls `flushAllInboundDebouncers({ timeoutMs: 5000 })` and prints the timing and what each `onFlush` actually received.
  3. `node_modules/.bin/tsx proof-pr46303-drain.mts` — driver that exercises `waitForFollowupQueueDrain` directly against the live `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` `Map` for three cases: empty registry, busy queue that drains at ~120ms, and a queue that stays busy past the timeout.
  4. `pnpm test src/auto-reply/inbound.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/queue/drain-all.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.test.ts` — full targeted suites for all three changed files.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal capture from running the patched modules in this worktree.

  Live CLI build proves the binary contains the patched code:

  ```
  $ pnpm openclaw --version
  OpenClaw 2026.5.4 (5d1e659)
  ```

  `flushAllInboundDebouncers` console output — 5 enqueued items across 2 debouncers and 3 keys, flushed in 1ms instead of waiting 2500ms:

  ```
  [before flush] A delivered=0, B delivered=0 (still buffered)
  [flushAllInboundDebouncers] flushedCount=3 elapsed=1ms (vs 2500ms debounce)
  [after flush] A delivered groups=[["alice:hello","alice:there"],["bob:howdy"]]
  [after flush] B delivered groups=[["ping-1","ping-2"]]
  ```

  `waitForFollowupQueueDrain` console output — three live cases against the real `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` map, including the timeout path that returns `drained:false` with the right `remaining` count:

  ```
  [empty]         {"drained":true,"remaining":0} elapsed=0ms
  [busy->drained] {"drained":true,"remaining":0} elapsed=151ms
  [timeout]       {"drained":false,"remaining":2} elapsed=200ms
  ```

  Real test runner output for the changed files (vitest reports the actual function counts; pnpm `test-projects.mjs` shards by project):

  ```
  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  24 passed (24)
     Duration  2.21s
  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts
   Test Files  2 passed (2)
        Tests  71 passed (71)
     Duration  4.05s
  [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 11.96s
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: `flushAllInboundDebouncers` deterministically drains buffered Signal-style messages in 1 ms while the natural debounce window is 2500 ms, and reports the right buffer count (`flushedCount=3` for two keys in one debouncer plus one in another). `waitForFollowupQueueDrain` returns immediately when the registry is empty, observes the queue clearing in real time during the busy case (151 ms — one poll interval past the 120 ms cleanup), and correctly returns `drained:false` with `remaining=2` when the timeout fires before drain. The CLI banner reports `(5d1e659)` confirming the built binary contains the patched commit. With the SIGUSR1 hook in `src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts` calling these helpers in the right order before `markGatewayDraining()`, late inbound messages are pushed into followup queues and given up to 5 s to drain instead of being silently dropped.
- **What was not tested**: An end-to-end real-Signal SIGUSR1 reproduction was not executed on this machine (no signal-cli account paired here); the production restart path is exercised through `src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.test.ts`, but a live Signal → `config.patch` → restart roundtrip on real `signal-cli` was not run. Behavior under simultaneous restart-while-flushing on a hung `onFlush` (the registered handle is intentionally retained on flush failure for a future sweep) was not exercised live; that path is covered only by the unit tests in `src/auto-reply/inbound.test.ts`.

target.title: fix(claude-live): return updatedInput instead of deprecated behavior:allow for can_use_tool
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

OpenClaw 2026.6.8 is incompatible with `@anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.156`. Every tool call that goes through the permission/approval flow fails with a `ZodError` before the tool actually runs.

Claude Code 2.1.x tightened the `PermissionResult` schema. The new union accepts:
- `{ updatedInput: <record> }` — allow (with possibly-modified inputs)
- `{ behavior: "deny", message: <string> }` — deny with reason

OpenClaw's bridge was returning the older shape `{ behavior: "allow" }` (no `updatedInput`) in **two separate code paths**, which validates against neither branch and causes ZodError in both the Claude live-session flow and the native hook relay flow.

**Fixes both allow-response paths identified by the ClawSweeper review.**

## Evidence

### Fix 1 — Claude live-session path (`claude-live-session.ts:877`)

```diff
   response: allowed
     ? {
-        behavior: "allow",
+        updatedInput: input ?? {},
         ...(toolUseId ? { toolUseID: toolUseId } : {}),
       }
```

### Fix 2 — Native hook relay path (`native-hook-relay.ts:396`)

The codex adapter's `renderPermissionDecisionResponse` now returns `{ updatedInput: input }` for allow decisions, and `runNativeHookRelayPermissionRequest` passes `request.toolInput` to all three allow call sites:

```diff
-    renderPermissionDecisionResponse: (decision, message) => ({
+    renderPermissionDecisionResponse: (decision, message, input) => ({
       ...
       decision:
         decision === "allow"
-          ? { behavior: "allow" }
+          ? { updatedInput: input ?? {} }
```

All three allow call sites (allow-always cache hit, allow decision, allow-always new) now pass `request.toolInput` as the third argument.

### Test coverage

**cli-runner.spawn.test.ts** — 3 allow-path tests updated:

| Test case | Assertion |
|-----------|----------|
| exec policy full/no-ask | `updatedInput` equals `{ command: "ls" }` |
| no exec policy (default) | `updatedInput` equals `{ command: "echo hi" }` |
| YOLO bypassPermissions | `updatedInput` equals `{ command: "ls" }` |

**native-hook-relay.test.ts** — 8 allow-path assertions updated from `toEqual({ behavior: "allow" })` to `toMatchObject({ updatedInput: expect.any(Object) })`:

| Test | Assertion |
|------|----------|
| duplicate permission allow-always | `updatedInput: expect.any(Object)` |
| primary permission allow | `updatedInput: expect.any(Object)` |
| allow-always remembered | `updatedInput: expect.any(Object)` |
| deny path (unchanged) | `behavior: "deny"` |

All deny-path tests remain unchanged — Claude Code 2.1.x still accepts the `{ behavior: "deny", message }` branch.

### Files changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.ts` | replace `behavior: "allow"` with `updatedInput: input` |
| `src/agents/cli-runner.spawn.test.ts` | 3 assertions: `behavior` → `updatedInput` |
| `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts` | adapter type + codex impl + 3 call sites |
| `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts` | 8 assertions: `behavior: "allow"` → `updatedInput: expect.any(Object)` |

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix: correct loopback address detection across multiple subsystems
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Three loopback/private address validation bugs across different subsystems, all following the same pattern: address ranges that are incomplete or semantically wrong.

1. **diffs `isLoopbackClientIp`**: Only matches exact `127.0.0.1`, missing the full 127.0.0.0/8 loopback range. Container and sandbox environments commonly use other addresses like `127.0.0.2`.

2. **audit-extra `isLoopbackPublishHost`**: Same pattern — only matches exact `127.0.0.1` instead of the full 127.0.0.0/8 range.

3. **device-pair `isLoopbackHost`**: Treats `0.0.0.0` (INADDR_ANY — bind to all interfaces) and `::` (IPv6 unspecified) as loopback. These are NOT loopback addresses. The canonical implementation in `src/gateway/net.ts` explicitly excludes both.

## Why This Change Was Made

Each fix is minimal (1-2 lines) and follows the established patterns from the canonical implementations in `src/gateway/net.ts` and `packages/net-policy/src/ip.ts`.

**Intentionally NOT changed:**
- `model-auth isLocalBaseUrl()`: `0.0.0.0` is preserved because existing tests and sibling code (`llm-idle-timeout.ts`) treat it as a local provider endpoint for synthetic local auth. This is an established contract.
- `session-manager` loop bounds: The `<=` in `buildIndex()` and `getPersistedFileEntries()` is intentional — `rememberLeafControl()` anchors opaque records at `fileEntries.length`, and the tail pass is required to read them back.

## Evidence

- **diffs tests**: 10 test files, 135 tests all pass
- **audit-extra tests**: 6/6 pass (including `isLoopbackPublishHost` coverage)
- **net.test.ts `isLoopbackHost`**: 49 test cases pass, explicitly verifying `0.0.0.0` and `::` are NOT loopback in the canonical implementation

target.title: fix(auto-reply): use a chat_type-neutral group/channel intro opener
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Resolves #95645.

`buildGroupChatContext` is built for **both** `group` and `channel` ChatTypes (the caller gates on `isGroupChat = ChatType === "group" || ChatType === "channel"` in `get-reply-run`), but its opener hard-coded:

```
You are in a <provider> group chat.
```

For a **public channel** the authoritative inbound-meta block reports `chat_type: "channel"`, while `"group"` denotes a private channel / group DM. So the natural-language opener contradicts the trusted metadata, and "group chat" is the more salient signal. Agents key off it and emit `chat_type: group` (or `--chat-type group` to tools) for a public channel.

Because session keys are namespaced `…:mattermost:<direct|channel|group>:<peerId>:thread:<root>`, a wrong `group` for a public channel addresses a **non-existent** session and breaks downstream routing.

## Fix

Reword the opener so it no longer declares a `chat_type`:

```diff
-  lines.push(`You are in a ${providerLabel} group chat.`);
+  lines.push(`You are in a shared ${providerLabel} chat (not a 1:1 DM).`);
```

This is the neutral wording proposed in the issue. It is **provider-agnostic** — `buildGroupChatContext` is shared by all providers, and "shared chat (not a 1:1 DM)" holds for every non-DM conversation this intro fires for. The remaining `"group"` mentions in the function ("be a good group participant", "…sent to this group chat") are colloquial role/delivery guidance, not a type declaration, so they are intentionally left unchanged.

## Scope

Deliberately narrow: this is the system-prompt-intro half only. The deeper `chat_type`-derivation inconsistency for private channels (channel-type vs target-string inference) is tracked separately in #95646 and is **not** addressed here.

## What changed

- `src/auto-reply/reply/groups.ts` — neutral opener + rationale comment.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts` — updated expectation; **new regression test** asserting a public-channel (`ChatType: "channel"`) context opens neutrally and never says "… group chat".
- `src/auto-reply/reply.triggers.group-intro-prompts.cases.ts` — updated the Discord/WhatsApp/Telegram opener expectations (delivery-guidance text unchanged).
- `src/agents/prompt-composition.test.ts` — updated the Slack group opener invariant.
- `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/discord-group-codex-message-tool.md` — regenerated opener line in the committed Codex prompt snapshot.

## Evidence

- **Source-level repro:** the failing path is exactly as the issue describes — a Mattermost `O` (public) channel maps to `chat_type: "channel"`, which `get-reply-run` routes through `buildGroupChatContext`, which emitted the "group chat" opener that collides with the trusted metadata.
- **Focused test (added):** the new `groups.test.ts` case fails before the change (the channel opener contains "Mattermost group chat") and passes after (`You are in a shared Mattermost chat (not a 1:1 DM).`, and `not.toContain("You are in a Mattermost group chat.")`). The case/snapshot/invariant updates keep the rest of the suite green.
- **Live production observation:** this exact reword has been running in our deployment (`ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw` + a one-line `sed` over `get-reply-*.js`). Before the patch, threaded cron delivery on a public Mattermost channel fail-closed because the agent emitted `chat_type=group` and bound a non-existent `group:<channelId>` session; after the patch the agent reports `chat_type: channel` and threaded delivery routes correctly.

target.title: fix(wiki): wiki_get and wiki compile miss nested source files
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes issue where wiki vaults with nested source files (e.g.
`sources/audi/car.md`, `sources/trading/Qullamaggie/`) silently
fail on `wiki_get` and are not indexed by `wiki compile`. Only
flat files directly in top-level QUERY_DIRS are discovered. Any
real-world vault organized with subdirectories hits this bug.

## Why This Change Was Made

Two functions — `listWikiMarkdownFiles` (wiki_get runtime lookup)
and `collectMarkdownFiles` (wiki compile indexing) — both call
`fs.readdir` without `{ recursive: true }`. Adding the flag and
adjusting path construction via `entry.parentPath` fixes both.
No other code paths affected.

## User Impact

- Nested vault pages in all QUERY_DIRS now discoverable by
  `wiki_get` and indexed by `wiki compile`
- Test vault went 385 → 1,093 pages, 11 → hundreds of sources
- Backward compatible — flat vaults unaffected

## Evidence

Before:
```
$ wiki_get("sources/audi/car.md")
Wiki page not found: sources/audi/car.md
```

After:
```
$ wiki_get("sources/audi/car.md")
# Audi A4 B8.5 2.0 TDI — Complete Vehicle History
## 1. Vehicle Overview ...

$ wiki compile
Compiled wiki vault (1093 pages, 467 indexes updated).
```

## Test plan
- [x] `wiki_get` resolves nested paths across all QUERY_DIRS
- [x] `wiki compile` indexes nested files (385 → 1,093 pages)
- [x] `index.md` exclusion still works for nested index.md
- [x] Backward compatible — flat vaults unaffected
- [ ] CI: `pnpm test:extension memory-wiki`
- [ ] CI: `pnpm build && pnpm check`

🤖 AI-assisted: drafted by Clawed (OpenClaw agent). Bug found live
on a real vault, root cause traced via source inspection, fix
validated with before/after evidence. I stand behind the change.

target.title: fix(auth): restore OAuth-backed OpenAI batch audio transcription
target.body:
Fixes #96135

## What Problem This Solves

Users with OpenAI/Codex OAuth profiles configured for `tools.media.audio` could no longer perform batch audio transcription after updating to version 2026.6.9. The system threw:
```
Auth profile "[redacted]" uses oauth auth, but openai/openai-audio-transcriptions requires an OpenAI API key profile.
```

This was a regression: the same configuration worked in prior versions.

## Why This Change Was Made

PR #90793 ("Fix OpenAI audio auth to use API keys") explicitly set the `modelApi` for OpenAI batch audio to `openai-audio-transcriptions`. At the same time, commit `d92b3b5cc2` ("refactor: unify OpenAI provider identity") introduced `directOpenAIPlatformModelRequiresApiKey`, which forces `api-key` for every OpenAI API except `openai-chatgpt-responses`. The combination caused OAuth to be rejected for audio transcription.

The fix adds `openai-audio-transcriptions` to the OAuth-allowlist in `directOpenAIPlatformModelRequiresApiKey`, restoring the pre-PR-90793 behavior. It also corrects a boundary condition: `normalizeLowercaseStringOrEmpty(undefined)` returns `""` rather than `undefined`, so the check now compares against `""` instead of `undefined`.

Additionally, a new `core/doctor/openai-audio-oauth` health check is registered so that `openclaw doctor --lint` warns when OpenAI audio is configured without an explicit Platform API key, giving users a heads-up about potential compatibility limits.

No other provider auth rules were changed, no config options were added, and no extension code was modified.

## User Impact

Users who rely on OpenAI/Codex OAuth for batch audio transcription will see the feature work again after this fix. A doctor warning will appear if OpenAI audio is configured without a Platform API key, recommending either adding an API key or switching to a different provider. All other users (those with API keys or non-OpenAI audio providers) are unaffected.

## Evidence

Environment: Ubuntu 22.04, Node v22.23.0, pnpm v11.2.2, OpenClaw 2026.6.9 (cd7e3df1ea), worktree SHA cd7e3df1ea

Before fix (negative control — new regression test fails on old code):

```bash
$ git stash
$ pnpm test src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts
 FAIL  |agents| src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts > getApiKeyForModel > allows an explicit OpenAI OAuth profile for OpenAI audio transcription models
Error: Auth profile "openai:chatgpt" uses oauth auth, but openai/openai-audio-transcriptions requires an OpenAI API key profile.
```

After fix:

```bash
$ pnpm test src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  71 passed (71)

$ pnpm test src/media-understanding/runner.auto-audio.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  9 passed (9)

$ pnpm test src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  19 passed (19)
```

Lint/format clean:

```bash
$ npx oxfmt --check src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.auto-audio.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

$ npx oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.auto-audio.test.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
```

Tests

| File | Tests | Status |
|------|-------|--------|
| `src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts` | 71 (1 new regression) | ✅ All pass |
| `src/media-understanding/runner.auto-audio.test.ts` | 9 | ✅ All pass |
| `src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts` | 19 (4 new) | ✅ All pass |
