target.title: Guard Skill Workshop update applies against delta drafts
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a Skill Workshop update-apply footgun where an update proposal that contains a delta/proposal note can be applied as the full replacement `SKILL.md`.

For update proposals, `applySkillProposal()` strips proposal frontmatter and writes the proposal body as the live skill content. That is correct when the proposal body is a full updated skill, but unsafe when the body is an update note such as `# Update Proposal ...` with "proposed changes" instructions. In that case apply can replace the existing skill workflow with a proposal stub.

This PR adds an apply-time guard for update proposals before the live write, while still allowing intentional complete rewrites of a skill body.

## What Problem This Solves

Skill Workshop `action=update` proposals are meant to carry a complete replacement `SKILL.md`. Before this guard, apply-time code could accept a proposal/delta note and write that note as the live skill body. That risks destroying the actual skill procedure.

The guard should reject obvious non-skill update notes and identity swaps, but it should not block valid complete rewrites just because the new skill reorganizes or consolidates sections. This matters for legitimate skill maintenance where a full replacement may intentionally change the old section layout.

## What changed

- Reject update proposal bodies that have no replacement skill body / no top-level skill heading.
- Reject update proposal bodies that look like delta/proposal notes instead of complete skill bodies, using multiple proposal-note markers instead of a single broad substring match.
- Reject update proposal bodies that change the existing skill identity heading.
- Allow complete replacement `SKILL.md` bodies that intentionally reorganize or consolidate old `##` sections.
- Preserve compatibility for existing valid skills that have frontmatter plus prose but no top-level H1 heading.
- Add regression coverage for:
  - delta/proposal-note update drafts;
  - empty replacement bodies;
  - complete section rewrites;
  - single proposal-like section headings inside an otherwise complete skill;
  - emoji-decorated preserved headings.

## Duplicate/adjacent work check

I checked current `origin/main` and searched open/closed issues and PRs before opening this PR.

Not duplicates:

- #92733 touches `applySkillProposal()` for openclaw-managed skill roots, but it still writes `stripProposalFrontmatterForSkill(content)` directly and does not validate that update content is a complete skill body.
- #94315 / #94249 cover large-proposal apply timeout behavior.
- #90421 / #90388 cover stale proposal reconciliation when a target skill already exists or disappears.
- #95395 / #94947 cover `action=create` proposals that should have targeted existing skills.

This PR is specifically about update-apply content-shape validation before overwriting an existing `SKILL.md`.

## Evidence

Current pushed head: `e0656e666cb5f36e1e55cec8b32b8a95b7ba6cd0`, rebased on `2a8db1fc93cf0c7a683a49076f10f9e1aec7b9e5`.

Exact validation run after the latest fix:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
git diff --check
```

Observed results on the exact pushed head:

```text
src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts: 30 passed
tsgo core-test: passed
git diff --check: passed
```

Real apply-time proof outside the unit test shard:

```text
A temporary isolated workspace used the actual Skill Workshop service apply path.

bad delta-note update -> rejected
original SKILL.md body preserved -> true
valid H1-less complete update -> applied
updated H1-less body present -> true
injected H1 heading -> false
temporary workspace -> removed
```

Redacted terminal output from that proof:

```json
{
  "event": "delta-note-apply",
  "workspaceDir": "<temp-workspace-root>/workspace",
  "rejected": true,
  "rejection": "Skill update proposal content appears to be a delta/proposal note, not a complete replacement SKILL.md. Revise the proposal with the full updated skill body before applying.",
  "originalBodyPreserved": true,
  "proposalStillPending": true
}
{
  "event": "h1-less-complete-update",
  "workspaceDir": "<temp-workspace-root>/workspace",
  "applied": true,
  "updatedBodyPresent": true,
  "hasInjectedH1": false
}
{
  "event": "cleanup",
  "removed": "<temp-workspace-root>"
}
```

Additional behavior covered by the focused test shard:

```text
empty replacement body -> rejected with the existing SKILL.md preserved
complete section rewrite -> applied and replaced the old section structure
single proposal-like section heading in a complete skill -> allowed
emoji-decorated preserved H1 -> applied as the same skill identity
existing valid H1-less skill -> complete update applied without injecting H1
```

What was not tested:

- Full repository CI is expected to run on GitHub for the pushed head.

target.title: fix(subagents): credit requester-consumed descendant completions
target.body:
## Summary
This PR fixes a lifecycle/delivery accounting gap for background and cron-owned subagent trees: when a requester actually consumes completed descendant subagent output, the descendant run is now durably credited as delivered instead of later being finalized as failed/suspended solely because no separate visible delivery event occurred.

## What Problem This Solves
Requester-owned subagent trees can legitimately consume descendant output without sending a separate descendant-visible delivery event. This is common for cron/background flows where a parent/requester gathers frozen child output, composes its own result, and then delivers that requester result outward. Before this PR, the descendant completion could remain delivery-unproven even though its output had already been consumed by the requester. Later cleanup/finalization could then classify that descendant as failed or suspended, producing delivery state that did not match real behavior. This PR gives the registry an explicit durable signal for that case: requester consumption is delivery evidence only for the exact descendant runs that were consumed, under the matching requester session/run-start window, when the requester path adopts descendant fallback output, so the consumed descendant rows do not later announce or finalize as pending just because the final outward send fails.

## Linked Context
- Related issue: #92433 — subagent completion can be silently dropped when an announce handoff reaches a requester transcript but the requester run ends before consuming it. This PR addresses the adjacent durable-accounting problem for completions that *are* consumed by a requester.
- Related issue: #67777 — subagent completion delivery can be lost on direct-announce timeout, drain, or orphan prune. This PR does not implement the full durable inbox/spool requested there, but it narrows one delivery-accounting gap by recording proven requester consumption.
- Related issue: #86537 — suspended final delivery can expire and discard a pending subagent completion. This PR keeps requester-consumed completions from remaining indistinguishable from undelivered completions in finalization state.
- Related issue: #90299 — subagent lifecycle state can report lost active execution context while still surfacing child output. This PR contributes to lifecycle/state consistency when descendant output is actually consumed.
- Related PR: #92934 — required assistant acknowledgement for completion wake handoffs, so transcript commit alone is not treated as requester consumption. This PR complements that by adding durable credit for explicit consumed descendant run IDs.
- Related PR: #35080 — deterministic subagent announce delivery with descendant gating. This PR builds on that direction by making consumed descendant accounting durable.
- Related PR: #44205 — stale descendant cron fallback summaries. This PR keeps the same stale-vs-current discipline for requester-consumed descendant credit.
- Related PR: #95604 — Discord subagent progress feedback. This PR is not a Discord UI change, but accurate delivered/failed lifecycle state is useful to progress/failure-marker consumers.
No exact existing issue/PR was found for `markDescendantCompletionConsumedByRequester` or this exact requester-consumed credit mechanism, so this PR should stand as the focused implementation thread for that behavior.

## Behavior change
When OpenClaw can prove that a requester consumed descendant subagent output, the matching descendant run receives durable delivery credit:
- `delivery.status` is set to `"delivered"` for the matched descendant completion;
- requester-consumption metadata is persisted with the run/delivery state;
- cleanup/finalize treats `delivery.status === "delivered"` as completed delivery proof;
- normal visible-delivery paths remain required for completions that were not consumed by a requester.
The matching is intentionally narrow. Credit is only applied when all of these are true:
- the consumed run IDs are explicit;
- the requester session key matches;
- the requester run-start window matches the consuming requester run;
- the consumption kind is known;
- cron fallback credit happens when cron adopts explicit descendant fallback output for the requester result;
- nested subagent announcement credit only covers direct child run IDs that were actually included in the requester-consumed output.

## Implementation notes
Core registry/runtime changes:
- Adds requester-consumed delivery metadata to subagent registry/delivery state.
- Adds a registry operation for marking descendant completions consumed by requester.
- Persists/restores the new delivery fields.
- Exposes the new runtime operation through the existing registry runtime shims.
Main files:
- `src/agents/subagent-registry-consumption.ts`
  - Implements durable requester-consumption credit.
  - Matches by requester session, explicit run IDs, and run-start window.
  - Records requester-consumption metadata and persists the updated run state.
- `src/agents/subagent-registry.types.ts`
  - Adds delivery/requester-consumption state fields.
- `src/agents/subagent-delivery-state.ts`
  - Persists and restores the new delivery fields.
- `src/agents/subagent-registry.ts`
- `src/agents/subagent-registry-runtime.ts`
- `src/agents/subagent-announce.registry.runtime.ts`
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-subagent-registry.runtime.ts`
  - Wires the new registry operation through runtime boundaries.
Requester-consumption paths:
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/subagent-followup.ts`
  - Reads completed descendant fallback replies and returns both formatted fallback text and the consumed descendant run IDs.
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts`
  - Applies cron fallback consumption credit when cron adopts explicit descendant fallback output, before the final outbound send.
- `src/agents/subagent-announce.ts`
  - Credits nested/direct child completions when their output is included in the requester-consumed announcement/wake result.
  - Uses the same latest-child dedupe input for rendering and consumed-run credit so stale child rows are not credited.
- `src/agents/subagent-announce-output.ts`
  - Keeps latest-child dedupe reusable while preserving caller-specific fields such as `runId`.

## Tests added/updated
Coverage added or extended for:
- positive requester-consumed descendant credit;
- no credit for mismatched requester session;
- no credit outside the requester run-start window;
- no credit without explicit consumed run IDs;
- persisted/restored requester-consumption delivery fields;
- cron fallback credit only after successful outbound delivery;
- cleanup/finalize treating `delivery.status === "delivered"` as proof;
- nested/direct child announcement credit;
- stale-vs-current child dedupe, including a negative assertion that stale child run IDs are not credited when stale output is not rendered.
Primary test files:
- `src/agents/subagent-registry-consumption.test.ts`
- `src/agents/subagent-delivery-state.test.ts`
- `src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.test.ts`
- `src/agents/subagent-registry.lifecycle-retry-grace.e2e.test.ts`
- `src/agents/subagent-announce.format.e2e.test.ts`
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/subagent-followup.test.ts`
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts`

## Evidence

### Rebase/squash proof — 2026-06-23 UTC

Latest pushed commit after same-base rebase and squash: `9ed14d33a2d980791fe3ea2349377d9e1160e328`.

All six coordinated PR branches in this batch were rebased onto the same pinned upstream base commit:

```text
dc9c11be917ebdc711b956250aa80a8e5b47bea6
```

Post-rebase local verification included conflict-marker/diff hygiene plus focused branch-specific gates. Final proof marker for this PR:

```text
POST_REBASE_20260623_REQUESTER_CONSUMED_GATES_OK
```
Focused validation run after the clean rebase and final nested-child dedupe fix:
```bash
pnpm tsgo:core:test --pretty false
pnpm tsgo:extensions:test --pretty false
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs   src/agents/subagent-announce.format.e2e.test.ts   src/agents/subagent-registry-consumption.test.ts   src/agents/subagent-delivery-state.test.ts   src/cron/isolated-agent/subagent-followup.test.ts   src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts
git diff --check
```
Result:
- Type checks passed.
- Focused Vitest validation passed across 4 shards.
- 201 focused tests passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.

## Expected semantics
This PR keeps the delivery model explicit:
- if a descendant result was visibly delivered, it is delivered;
- if a requester consumed the descendant output because cron adopted it as the requester result, the descendant is delivered by requester consumption;
- if neither happened, failure/suspension handling still applies.

## Risk and compatibility
Risk is moderate because this touches subagent lifecycle/delivery finalization, but the implementation is deliberately constrained:
- no broad “assume delivered” fallback was added;
- no delivery status is credited without explicit consumed run IDs;
- matching is scoped to requester session and requester run-start timing;
- cron fallback credit is gated behind explicit adopted descendant fallback output;
- stale nested child rows are explicitly excluded after latest-child dedupe;
- persisted state changes are additive.
The main compatibility consideration is that existing delivery state can simply lack the new requester-consumption fields; restore logic treats those fields as optional.

## PR/Commit Surface Breakdown
Diff surface against `origin/main...HEAD` after the clean rebase:
- Source: 11 files, +212 / -51
- Tests: 7 files, +232 / -5
Total: 18 files, +444 / -56.

## Reviewer checklist
Recommended review focus:
- `src/agents/subagent-registry-consumption.ts`
  - Verify matching is narrow enough: run IDs, requester session key, and run-start window.
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts`
  - Verify cron fallback credit is only applied after successful requester outbound delivery.
- `src/agents/subagent-announce.ts`
  - Verify nested/direct child credit only uses deduped, actually-consumed child rows.
- `src/agents/subagent-delivery-state.ts`
  - Verify persistence/restore behavior is additive and safe for older state.
- Tests listed above, especially negative cases around mismatched requester, stale child rows, and no explicit consumed run IDs.

## PR/Commit Surface Breakdown

Source: 12 files, +192 / -44
Tests: 6 files, +251 / -12
Total: 18 files, +443 / -56

## Rebase / Proof Refresh (2026-06-23)

- Base: `654544b6b7c49d78afbacacd4861bcc830172780`
- Head after refresh: `808e4f2654fd259f5c401098d34c546758256574`
- Branch: `kklouzal/openclaw:p1/requester-consumed-completion-credit-clean`
- Conflict resolution: None for the follow-up refresh. Squashed the existing requester-consumed PR changes plus the cron/SQLite follow-up into one coherent commit.
- Behavior proof refreshed: cron now credits `markDescendantCompletionConsumedByRequester` when it adopts explicit descendant fallback output, before final direct delivery, so a failed final outward send does not leave already-consumed descendant rows pending. SQLite persistence now derives `pending_final_delivery` only from active delivery statuses (`pending`, `in_progress`, `suspended`), so delivered/requester-consumed rows that retain payload metadata do not rehydrate as pending.
- Validation:
  - `git diff --check` -> passed.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.store.sqlite.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-consumption.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/subagent-followup.test.ts src/agents/subagent-delivery-state.test.ts` -> passed, 5 files / 124 tests across 3 shards.
  - Follow-up #95996 settlement check in its local worktree: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts -- -t 'yield|settle|descendant'` -> passed, 6 selected tests / 59 skipped.
- Push: force-with-lease to `kklouzal/openclaw:p1/requester-consumed-completion-credit-clean` completed.

target.title: fix: keep yielded parent runs deferred until subagents settle
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where parent-agent runs that spawned child sessions/subagents and then called `sessions_yield` could look finished before the parent task actually completed.

The most visible failures were:

- isolated cron `agentTurn` jobs with valid delivery targets could finish as `ok / not-delivered` because the yielded parent had no final payload yet;
- Discord source-message lifecycle could clear parent progress/done indicators while child work was still active or while the parent was waiting to reconcile final output;
- run history/status surfaces made yielded parent work look like delivery failure, ordinary success with no output, or completed source-message work instead of a paused/resumable parent lifecycle.

`session_yield` is supposed to end the current model turn, not the parent task. This PR makes that distinction explicit for the affected runtime consumers.

## Why This Change Was Made

This change promotes yielded-parent semantics into a shared parent lifecycle classifier and routes the affected cron, subagent-registry, and Discord/source-message paths through that state instead of letting each consumer infer completion from terminal-shaped model-attempt events, missing final text, or abort-shaped control flow.

The shipped behavior is:

- yielded parent + active descendants is treated as waiting/deferred, not ordinary completion;
- yielded parent + no descendant is treated as diagnostic error;
- cron delivery waits for a real final outcome instead of counting the yielded attempt as `ok / not-delivered`;
- Discord/source-message progress remains parent-owned while the yielded parent is waiting;
- descendant settlement wakes/reconciles yielded parents through the existing continuation paths.

## User Impact

Operators and developers can now rely on `sessions_yield` after spawning child/subagent work without the parent run being misrepresented as complete at the cron or Discord lifecycle boundary.

Practically:

- autonomous development crons stop inflating misleading `ok / not-delivered` results for intentionally yielded runs;
- cron history exposes a distinct `deferred` state for yielded parent handoff;
- Discord progress no longer marks the parent source message done just because the parent yielded while children continue;
- yielded parent runs are resumed/reconciled when descendant work settles;
- debugging points to the parent/child lifecycle state instead of falsely implying delivery target resolution failed.

## Linked Context

- #95535 — `sessions_yield` isolated cron resumption/delivery gap.
- #92369 — subagent orchestration in isolated cron sessions needs reliable spawn/wait/aggregate semantics.
- #92116 — `sessions_yield` completion delivered as passive context can break orchestration loops.
- #86537 — subagent terminal reconciliation can expire suspended final delivery.
- #86538 — session write-lock timeouts can block subagent delivery lanes.
- #95833 — subagent abort-settle/session-lock failure can break session continuation.
- #92460 — isolated cron completion delivery gap around final controller return.
- #95604 — Discord subagent progress visibility.
- #94153 — durable queued subagent completion announcements.
- #95847 — requester-consumed descendant completion accounting.

## Implementation Summary

### Shared parent lifecycle state

- Adds `src/agents/agent-lifecycle-parent-state.ts`.
- Classifies lifecycle events with explicit yielded/waiting vs terminal semantics.
- Exports the classifier through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/reply-runtime`.
- Routes subagent registry and Discord source-message handling through the classifier instead of local `yielded && paused` inference.

### Cron lifecycle and delivery

- Adds first-class cron run status `deferred`.
- Classifies yielded descendant handoff before delivery-mode branching, so text, structured payloads, `delivery.mode: none`, source-satisfied delivery, and normal direct delivery all share the same semantics.
- Prevents duplicate/interim parent announcements while descendants are active.
- Records yielded-without-descendant as explicit error diagnostic.
- Keeps deferred manual task runs active rather than failing them.
- Disables one-shot deferred cron jobs after handoff to avoid retry loops.

### Persistence, protocol, CLI, and UI

- Extends run-log JSON/SQLite encoding and status filtering for `deferred`.
- Extends Gateway protocol schemas and cron server filters.
- Extends CLI status/wait typing and docs.
- Extends hook-visible status typing.
- Adds Control UI status/filter labels and generated locale fallbacks.

### Parent/subagent reconciliation

- Tracks yielded/paused parents that still have pending descendants.
- Marks yielded parents with `wakeOnDescendantSettle` so they are resumed/reconciled when descendant work settles.
- Handles child success/failure settlement through existing subagent lifecycle recovery paths.
- Adds regression coverage for yielded parents being reconciled after child failure so diagnostics can wake the parent.

### Discord/source-message lifecycle

- Projects lifecycle events from agent execution into reply/channel handling.
- Uses the shared classifier to identify yielded/waiting parent state.
- Preserves in-progress/progress representation for yielded parents with active children.
- Avoids prematurely marking Discord status reactions done or clearing draft/progress state when the parent yielded and remains waiting.

### Concurrent user-message semantics

`docs/tools/subagents.md` documents the selected policy: if a human sends another message while a requester is yielded, the new message starts a normal turn for the same requester session. OpenClaw keeps the yielded run visible as waiting on descendant completion rather than converting it into a stale final reply; when descendant completion arrives, the completion event is handed back to the requester for synthesis.

## Evidence

### Rebase/squash proof — 2026-06-23 UTC

Latest pushed commit after same-base rebase and squash: `a8aa8bfb134e2b7d5ce77510dc076c13dfd0e4a0`.

All six coordinated PR branches in this batch were rebased onto the same pinned upstream base commit:

```text
dc9c11be917ebdc711b956250aa80a8e5b47bea6
```

Post-rebase local verification included conflict-marker/diff hygiene plus focused branch-specific gates. Final proof marker for this PR:

```text
POST_REBASE_20260623_YIELD_LIFECYCLE_SDK_SURFACE_REMEDIATED_OK
```

### Focused test coverage

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/agent-lifecycle-parent-state.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry.steer-restart.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry-queries.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts \
  src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts \
  ui/src/ui/views/cron.test.ts \
  src/cron/service/ops.test.ts \
  src/cron/service.persists-delivered-status.test.ts \
  src/cron/run-log.test.ts \
  packages/gateway-protocol/src/cron-validators.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts
```

Passed.

Coverage includes:

- shared parent lifecycle classifier behavior;
- yielded cron parent + active descendants -> `deferred`;
- yielded cron parent + no descendant -> diagnostic `error`;
- structured/no-delivery/source-satisfied delivery paths classified before delivery;
- no duplicate parent interim delivery while descendants are active;
- deferred run-log persistence and filtering;
- deferred Gateway protocol validators;
- manual `cron.run` deferred task-ledger behavior;
- one-shot deferred cron disabling;
- Control UI deferred status/filter rendering;
- CLI wait/status behavior for deferred;
- yielded parent descendant-settle wake/reconciliation;
- yielded parent child-failure diagnostics;
- Discord yielded-parent progress preservation;
- plugin SDK subpath export coverage for the classifier.

### Type, docs, and formatting gates

```bash
pnpm ui:i18n:check
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:test:src --noEmit
pnpm tsgo:test:ui
pnpm tsgo:test:packages --noEmit
pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/tools/subagents.md
git diff --check
```

Passed.

Final local gate marker:

```text
FINAL_PR_READY_YIELD_LIFECYCLE_GATES_OK
```

## PR/Commit Surface Breakdown

Source: 63 files, +470 / -111
Tests: 10 files, +401 / -15
Total: 73 files, +871 / -126


### Live Dev-01 behavior proof — 2026-06-23 UTC

Runtime: Dev-01 installed stack commit `5c1fd341c89b1493fed1ea6014388394291450c3` via `/home/kklouzal/update.sh`; `openclaw --version` reported `OpenClaw 2026.6.9 (5c1fd34)` and `openclaw-gateway.service` was active from that stack.

Focused live cron/subagent handoff run:

```bash
openclaw cron add --json \
  --name pr95996-95847-live-20260623T222458Z \
  --at +6h --keep-after-run --session isolated --no-deliver \
  --tools sessions_spawn,sessions_yield --timeout-seconds 600 \
  --message 'Live proof marker pr95996-95847-live-20260623T222458Z ...'

openclaw cron run 514fd6e1-862e-43ca-87e3-da1197a7c938 \
  --wait --wait-timeout 12m --poll-interval 5s --timeout 600000
```

Redacted observed output from the live run:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "enqueued": true,
  "runId": "manual:514fd6e1-862e-43ca-87e3-da1197a7c938:1782253518340:1",
  "completed": true,
  "status": "deferred",
  "run": {
    "status": "deferred",
    "diagnostics": {
      "summary": "cron isolated agent yielded while descendant runs are still active",
      "entries": [
        {
          "source": "delivery",
          "severity": "warn",
          "message": "cron isolated agent yielded while descendant runs are still active"
        }
      ]
    },
    "deliveryStatus": "not-requested",
    "delivery": { "fallbackUsed": false, "delivered": false },

}
```

The non-zero wait exit is expected for this proof: `openclaw cron run --wait` exits non-zero for persisted `deferred` status, while still returning the run record. Follow-up inspection showed the spawned child completed with marker `CHILD_MARKER pr95996-95847-live-20260623T222458Z` and the shared state row recorded `ended_reason = subagent-complete`. The cron shared-state row for the same job persisted `last_run_status = deferred`, `last_delivery_status = not-requested`, and diagnostic summary `cron isolated agent yielded while descendant runs are still active`.

Proof sources:

Remaining proof gap: this live proof covers the cron yielded-parent/subagent deferred lifecycle path. It does not provide Discord visual status-reaction proof.

- Head after refresh: `31d2476f773adc571c95196e4d51ff81a3215265`

target.title: [AI] fix(daemon): set default NODE_OPTIONS --max-old-space-size in gateway service env
target.body:
## Summary

Gateway daemon services installed by `openclaw daemon install` now receive a default `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192`, preventing V8 OOM crash-loops on larger workspaces (~30 agents, cron workflows).
- Problem: Node 22+ defaults V8 heap to ~4 GB; the gateway exceeds this during startup on workspaces with many agents, sessions, and cron workflows, causing persistent crash-loops (2,314 restarts/day).
- Solution: Set `--max-old-space-size=8192` in the service environment during daemon install, matching the existing `BUILD_MAX_OLD_SPACE_MB` constant used by the update runner.
- What changed: `src/daemon/service-env.ts` (new `resolveServiceNodeOptions` helper, plumbed into `buildCommonServiceEnvironment`), `src/daemon/service-env.test.ts` (+7 test cases).
- What did NOT change: Runtime behavior, config schema, CLI interface, or existing installations (only new installs/reinstalls pick up the default).

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #96203
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

Users with medium-to-large workspaces (~30 agents, 12 cron jobs) experience persistent gateway crash-loops because the launchd plist / systemd unit generated by `openclaw daemon install` omits `NODE_OPTIONS`. Node 22+ caps V8 old-space at ~4 GB, and the gateway's startup memory spike exceeds this limit. The workaround (manually editing the plist to add `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192`) is not discoverable. This fix makes the default heap size match the update runner's existing `BUILD_MAX_OLD_SPACE_MB` (8192 MB), which is already a known-good value within the codebase.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Gateway daemon services installed via `openclaw daemon install` lacked `NODE_OPTIONS`, causing OOM crashes on larger workspaces.
- Real environment tested: macOS 26.3 (Darwin arm64), Node v26.0.0, `fix/gateway-daemon-heap-96203` branch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/service-env.test.ts --run

$ node --input-type=module -e "
import { buildServiceEnvironment } from './src/daemon/service-env.js';
const env = buildServiceEnvironment({ env: { HOME: '/home/user' }, port: 18789 });
console.log('NODE_OPTIONS:', env.NODE_OPTIONS);
"
```

- Evidence after fix:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/service-env.test.ts --run
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  95 passed (95)

$ node --input-type=module -e "
import { buildServiceEnvironment } from './src/daemon/service-env.js';
const env = buildServiceEnvironment({ env: { HOME: '/home/user' }, port: 18789 });
console.log('NODE_OPTIONS:', env.NODE_OPTIONS);
"
NODE_OPTIONS: --max-old-space-size=8192
```

Service environment matrix:

| Input env | Expected NODE_OPTIONS |
|-----------|----------------------|
| `{}` (no NODE_OPTIONS) | `--max-old-space-size=8192` |
| `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=16384 --no-warnings` | `--max-old-space-size=16384 --no-warnings` (preserved) |
| `NODE_OPTIONS=--no-warnings --experimental-vm-modules` | `--no-warnings --experimental-vm-modules --max-old-space-size=8192` (appended) |
| `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB=6144` | `--max-old-space-size=6144` |
| `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB=not-a-number` | `--max-old-space-size=8192` (fallback) |
| `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB=0` | `undefined` (disabled) |

- Observed result after fix:
  1. `buildServiceEnvironment` now includes `NODE_OPTIONS: "--max-old-space-size=8192"` by default
  2. Existing `NODE_OPTIONS` with `--max-old-space-size` are preserved unchanged
  3. `NODE_OPTIONS` with other flags get `--max-old-space-size` appended
  4. `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB` overrides the default; zero disables the flag entirely
  5. Invalid `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB` falls back to the default
  6. `buildNodeServiceEnvironment` also includes the default `NODE_OPTIONS`
- What was not tested: Actual macOS launchd plist generation with `openclaw daemon install` end-to-end (requires a real macOS environment with launchctl); systemd unit generation on Linux; Windows Scheduled Task path (Scheduled Tasks inherit PATH from task host, so Windows is unaffected).

## Root Cause

Node 22+ changed the default V8 heap limit to `min(host RAM × 0.5, 4 GB)`. The gateway's startup memory profile (session state hydration, plugin manifest loading, cron registry initialization) exceeds the 4 GB ceiling on workspaces with many agents and cron jobs. The `openclaw daemon install` command generated launchd plists / systemd units without `NODE_OPTIONS`, relying on the Node default.

The codebase already recognizes this: `BUILD_MAX_OLD_SPACE_MB = 8192` in `src/infra/update-runner.ts` sets the same flag during the self-update build step, but the daemon install path was never updated to match.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New `openclaw daemon install` / `openclaw gateway install` runs now embed `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192` in the generated service definition.
- Users who set `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB` can customize the heap size (e.g., `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB=6144`).
- Setting `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB=0` disables the heap flag and falls back to Node defaults.
- Existing installations are not affected until the user re-runs `daemon install`.

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: All 7 NODE_OPTIONS test variants pass; existing 88 service-env tests continue to pass.
- Edge cases checked: Empty NODE_OPTIONS, existing --max-old-space-size, OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB zero/invalid/valid, node service environment.
- What you did NOT verify: Cross-platform daemon install E2E (macOS launchctl, Linux systemd).

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — existing `NODE_OPTIONS` with `--max-old-space-size` are preserved; no breaking change.
- [x] Config/env changes? Yes — new `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB` env var (optional override).
- [x] Migration needed? No — only affects new installs/reinstalls.

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes. Setting the heap default at the daemon install boundary is the right layer — it protects all managed gateway processes (launchd, systemd, schtasks) without touching runtime code, config schema, or the CLI surface.
- **Refactor needed**: No.
- **Alternative considered**: Adding a `--heap-mb` CLI flag to `openclaw daemon install`. This would be a user-friendly addition but adds complexity (flag parsing, validation, help text) for a value that 95% of users should never need to change. The `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB` env var offers equivalent control with zero CLI changes.

## AI Assistance 🤖

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

**Highest risk**: Setting `--max-old-space-size=8192` on machines with ≤8 GB total RAM could cause host swapping. Mitigation: the 8 GB default matches the existing `BUILD_MAX_OLD_SPACE_MB` constant already used in production update builds; users on memory-constrained hosts can set `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB` to a lower value or `0` to opt out.

---

Fixes #96203

target.title: feat(browser): add browserbase profile driver with per-attach connectUrl resolution
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a new `browserbase` profile driver to the OpenClaw browser plugin. Profiles using this driver declare a Browserbase keep-alive session id and an env var name for the API key; on every CDP attach OpenClaw fetches a fresh `connectUrl` from `GET /v1/sessions/<id>` and hands it to the existing CDP pipeline. Eliminates the stale-signing-key fragility that breaks stateful Browserbase work today.

```jsonc
"browserbase-lexzur": {
  "driver": "browserbase",
  "browserbaseSessionId": "44589bac-33f0-4080-9eff-f1b3e3a0bf9c",
  "browserbaseApiKeyEnv": "BROWSERBASE_API_KEY",
  "attachOnly": true
}
```

## Why

The existing `cdpUrl: "wss://...?signingKey=..."` model puts a short-lived JWT into static config. Browserbase rotates the signing key well before the session itself expires — verified empirically with two consecutive `GET /v1/sessions/<id>` calls returning different `signingKey` values for the same session. Once the embedded key ages out, every CDP attach gets `1008 unauthorized` and any multi-step stateful workflow (login → multi-step → submit) breaks mid-flow.

The fix is the documented Browserbase pattern: fetch a fresh `connectUrl` via the Sessions API on each attach. Bearer auth comes from the operator's own credentials, refreshed on each request, no embedded lifetime to track.

## What changed

### New module — `extensions/browser/src/browser/browserbase-session.ts`

- `fetchBrowserbaseConnectUrl(sessionId, apiKeyEnv, opts)` — issues `GET /v1/sessions/<id>` with `X-BB-API-Key`. 8s default timeout, combines internal AbortController with caller-supplied `signal`. Three named error classes: `BrowserbaseAuthConfigError`, `BrowserbaseSessionUnavailableError`, `BrowserbaseSessionMalformedError`.
- `withResolvedCdpUrl(profile, opts)` — identity for non-browserbase drivers (same reference returned, zero allocations, no fetch); shallow-copy with freshly resolved `cdpUrl` / `cdpHost` / `cdpIsLoopback: false` for browserbase drivers.
- **No caching.** Two calls = two HTTP requests by design — the whole point is signing-key rotation.

### Resolver branch — `extensions/browser/src/browser/config.ts`

- `resolveProfile()` gains a `browserbase` branch that validates `browserbaseSessionId` is UUID-shaped and `browserbaseApiKeyEnv` matches `/^[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*$/`. Returns `cdpUrl: ""` — URL is resolved lazily at attach time.
- Operator-friendly fail-fast at config load (not on first hot-path attach) for missing/malformed fields.

### Capability branch — `extensions/browser/src/browser/profile-capabilities.ts`

- Third branch for `driver === "browserbase"` → `mode: "remote-cdp"`, `isRemote: true`, `usesPersistentPlaywright: true`, all `/json/*` endpoint flags off (Browserbase doesn't expose JSON tab endpoints; everything goes through the signed WS connectUrl).

### Wired into CDP attach call sites

- `server-context.tab-ops.ts` — `readTabs` + `openTab` Playwright legs.
- `server-context.selection.ts` — `focusTab` + `closeTab` Playwright legs.
- `server-context.availability.ts` — `isReachable` / `isHttpReachable` probe via cheap `fetchBrowserbaseConnectUrl` for browserbase profiles (instead of full CDP WS attach per status poll).

### Default-timeout bump (separate commit `700639f1df`)

Based on real-world transcontinental remote-CDP topology (SFO-hosted Browserbase + Linux gateway). Previous 1500/2000 defaults were too aggressive — left zero margin on healthy attaches under transit jitter.

- `remoteCdpTimeoutMs`: 1500 → 3000
- `remoteCdpHandshakeTimeoutMs` floor: `max(remoteCdpTimeoutMs*2, 5000)` → resolves to 6000 by default. Preserves the `handshake ≥ 2× http` invariant for operators who customize one without bumping the other.

### Docs (separate commit `68f68af6f7`)

New subsection in `docs/tools/browser.md` covering keep-alive sessions, auto-create vs session-id distinction, the `POST /v1/sessions` curl example with `keepAlive: true`, the new profile shape, why static-URL signingKey rotation is fragile, and the SecretRef follow-up note.

## Tests

```
extensions/browser/src/browser/browserbase-session.test.ts     13 passed
extensions/browser/src/browser/config.browserbase.test.ts       8 passed
```

Full extension-browser suite: **103 files, 1099 passed, 1 pre-existing skip, 0 failed.**

## Live smoke test

Verified end-to-end against the production Browserbase API with a fresh keepAlive session:

| Test | Result |
|---|---|
| Happy path live `GET /v1/sessions/<id>` | ✅ 584ms |
| No-caching contract (two calls = different signingKeys) | ✅ confirmed signing-key rotation working |
| `BrowserbaseAuthConfigError` raised on missing env var | ✅ |
| `withResolvedCdpUrl` shallow-copy preserves immutability | ✅ |
| Identity passthrough for non-browserbase drivers | ✅ same reference returned |

## Out of scope (deliberately)

- **SecretRef supported-surface expansion** for `browserbaseApiKeyEnv`. The new field is read from `process.env` at attach time, not via SecretRef resolution. Follow-up PR to add the path to the canonical credential surface. Operators who want a 1Password-only secret can inject the env var via `op run --env-file=...` at gateway start.
- **Auto-mint-on-stale-session fallback** — when the configured session id is no longer `RUNNING`, this PR fails loudly with `BrowserbaseSessionUnavailableError`. Auto-minting would mean OpenClaw writes its own config at runtime; that's a separate design conversation.
- **Renaming the legacy `browserbase` auto-create profile** to `browserbase-ephemeral`. Follow-up release with migration tooling.

## Risk + rollback

Risk surface is narrow — the new code path only activates when a profile declares `driver: "browserbase"`. Existing `openclaw` and `existing-session` profiles get an identity passthrough in `withResolvedCdpUrl` (same reference, zero allocations, no fetch). Default-timeout bump is conservative and only affects remote CDP profiles; loopback `openclaw` profiles are unaffected.

Rollback is a revert. Existing deployments without a `browserbase`-driver profile are bit-identical in behavior except for the +1.5s remote-CDP timeout bump.

## Lexzur audit context

Lexzur audit on 2026-05-17 burned 4-5 Browserbase sessions and as many OTPs trying to drive a multi-step SSO ticket form before identifying the static-signingKey-in-URL pattern as the root cause. This PR is the permanent fix for that class of failure — multi-step Browserbase flows can now complete in a single keep-alive session with cookies persisting across tool calls.

target.title: ci: add regression detector checkout gate
target.body:
## Summary
- add a dedicated regression detector gate workflow job for bug-fix evidence
- check out the repository before running the gate script so the CI job has repo files available
- require reviewer approval for market-sensitive fixes

## Evidence
Pre-fix reproduction command: RPAA-2350 identified the CI blocker where the regression detector gate ran without repository checkout/repo-file availability.

Post-fix verification command: `GITHUB_EVENT_PATH=<synthetic bug payload> node scripts/check-regression-detector-gate.mjs` passes with required evidence.

Regression test evidence command: synthetic missing-evidence and market-sensitive payloads fail as expected; `git diff --check` passes.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Regression detector CI gate can run from a real OpenClaw checkout and enforce required fix evidence before a PR proceeds.

Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw checkout at `/Users/minion/Documents/openclaw-rpaa-2350-regression-detector`, branch `fix/rpaa-2350-regression-detector-checkout`, Node from the local developer environment.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Checked the branch diff against current `origin/main`.
2. Ran the gate with a synthetic fix PR event containing required reproduction, verification, and regression evidence.
3. Ran the gate with a synthetic fix PR event missing the required evidence fields.
4. Ran the gate with a synthetic market-sensitive fix PR event missing the special approval label.

Evidence after fix:

```text
$ git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
(no output)

$ GITHUB_EVENT_PATH=<synthetic fix payload> node scripts/check-regression-detector-gate.mjs
PR classified as bug-fix: fix: repair regression detector gate checkout
Required regression evidence fields and checkboxes are present.

$ GITHUB_EVENT_PATH=<synthetic missing-evidence payload> node scripts/check-regression-detector-gate.mjs
Regression detector gate failed; missing required evidence:
- pre-fix failure/reproduction evidence
- post-fix verification evidence
- regression test evidence

$ GITHUB_EVENT_PATH=<synthetic market-sensitive payload> node scripts/check-regression-detector-gate.mjs
Regression detector gate failed; missing required evidence:
- special approval label for market-sensitive fix PR
```

Observed result after fix: The gate passes only when required fix evidence is present, fails when required evidence is missing, and fails market-sensitive fix payloads without the special approval label. The workflow job now performs `actions/checkout` before running the script, so the script is available in CI.

What was not tested: Full upstream merge was not tested locally; GitHub Actions is the source of truth for the full matrix.

Closes RPAA-2350.

target.title: Avoid writing plaintext profile keys to models.json
target.body:
## Summary

This keeps plaintext auth-profile API keys out of generated `models.json`.

The provider normalization path already preserves env and SecretRef-backed credentials as markers. Plaintext auth-profile keys could still be copied into the generated provider config when a provider had models but no configured `apiKey`, when a generated provider `apiKey` matched the plaintext profile key, or when merge mode preserved an existing `models.json` value after the first normalization pass.

This PR skips plaintext profile keys for `models.json` persistence, strips matching plaintext values during provider normalization, and re-normalizes after merge mode preserves existing provider fields. The strip also applies to merge-preserved provider entries with no model rows, so old plaintext profile keys do not survive just because `models` is empty. Unrelated user-managed provider keys stay intact.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: plaintext auth-profile API keys should not be persisted into generated `models.json` provider entries, including merge-mode regeneration from an existing `models.json` and merge-preserved provider entries with `models: []`.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Android/arm64 Termux with Node v24.13.0. I used a temp agent directory with a real `auth-profiles.json` entry containing a plaintext `openai:default` key. The merge-mode proof used an existing parsed `models.json` with an OpenAI provider whose `apiKey` matched that plaintext profile key, `models: []`, plus a separate custom provider with an unrelated user-managed key.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` script that saved the plaintext auth profile, called `planOpenClawModelsJsonWithDeps` in `models.mode = \"merge\"`, parsed the generated contents, and printed whether the merged OpenAI provider still had an `apiKey` property and whether the unrelated custom provider key was preserved.

Evidence after fix: terminal output from the proof command:

```json
{
  "action": "write",
  "openaiModelsLength": 0,
  "openaiHasApiKeyProperty": false,
  "openaiApiKeyValue": null,
  "customApiKeyPreserved": true,
  "providerIds": [
    "openai",
    "custom"
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: merge mode keeps the OpenAI and custom provider entries, removes the OpenAI `apiKey` value that matched the plaintext auth profile even though the OpenAI provider has no models, and preserves the unrelated custom provider `apiKey`.

What was not tested: full native `tsgo` in this environment, because `@typescript/native-preview-android-arm64` is unavailable. Plain `tsc` on the full core test project also ran out of heap locally.

## Tests

- `node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize-keys.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts src/agents/models-config.merge.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/models-config.plan.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.secret-helpers.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize-keys.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`


Addresses #11829

target.title: perf(agents): bootstrap context cache, PI model-discovery cache, auth-store option keys, tool-descriptor kill switch
target.body:
Rebased follow-up to #76348.

Since #76348 was filed, upstream landed the simpler half it proposed — the per-session bootstrap-files snapshot cache (`src/agents/bootstrap-cache.ts`'s `getOrLoadBootstrapFiles`). This PR isolates and re-bases the **still-relevant** gaps in that original PR on top of current `main`, dropping anything upstream now handles better.

#76348 is being closed in favor of this PR (its branch was 18.5k commits stale and not rebaseable in place).

## What's in this PR

### 1. Second-layer bootstrap **context** snapshot cache
`src/agents/bootstrap-cache.ts`, `src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts`

Upstream caches the raw `WorkspaceBootstrapFile[]` per session. This adds a second LRU on top that caches the full post-hook / filtered `{ bootstrapFiles, contextFiles }` payload returned by `resolveBootstrapContextForRun`. That payload is the expensive thing — hook overrides, completion filtering, bootstrap-context-mode filtering, char-budget enforcement.

- **What**: `readCachedBootstrapContext` / `writeCachedBootstrapContext`, LRU bounded at 128 entries, with a stable JSON cache key that mixes in sessionKey, workspaceDir + workspace mtime, contextMode, runKind, agentId/defaultAgentId, channelId, context-injection + bootstrap config, resolved max-char limits, a raw-file content fingerprint, the internal hook registry version, and the plugins config.
- **Why**: every embedded-runner turn currently re-runs the full hook + filter + char-budget pipeline even though inputs only change when files / config / hooks change.
- **Invalidation**: when `getOrLoadBootstrapFiles` detects a session's raw files changed, it calls `clearBootstrapContextSnapshotsForSession(sessionKey)`. Plus `getInternalHookRegistryVersion()` is mixed into the key so hook registration mutations bust the cache.
- **Kill switch**: `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BOOTSTRAP_CONTEXT_CACHE=1`.

### 2. PI model-discovery LRU cache
`src/agents/pi-model-discovery.ts`, `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.ts`

`discoverModels(authStorage, agentDir, options)` walks the agent dir, reads `models.json`, reads auth stores, and constructs a `PiModelRegistry`. It's invoked per `resolveModel` call. Upstream has zero LRU/discovery-cache references in this file.

- **What**: LRU bounded at 64 entries, keyed by agentDir + provider filter + normalize flag + relevant config slices (`config.models`, `config.plugins`) + auth-store/state file fingerprints + `models.json` file fingerprint. Invalidates automatically when any auth or models.json file is touched.
- **Why**: avoids rebuilding the registry for every turn when nothing has changed.
- **Wiring**: `resolveModel` and `resolveModelAsync` in `pi-embedded-runner/model.ts` now pass `{ config: cfg }` through so two agents with different model configs do NOT share a cache entry.
- **Kill switch**: `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_MODEL_DISCOVERY_CACHE=1`.
- **Test**: new `src/agents/pi-model-discovery.cache.test.ts` (4 cases: identity, mtime-busts-cache, distinct-options, kill switch).

### 3. Option-sensitive cache keys for the auth-profile store
`src/agents/auth-profiles/store.ts`, `src/agents/auth-profiles/store-cache.ts`

Upstream caches loaded auth-profile stores in a `Map<string, …>` keyed by `authPath`. The problem: two callers with different `LoadAuthProfileStoreOptions` (e.g. one with `syncExternalCli: true`, another with `syncExternalCli: false`) poison each other — caller A's overlaid-external-CLI store can leak to caller B reading the same path, and vice versa.

- **What**: `store-cache.ts` now takes an opaque `cacheKey: string` instead of `authPath`. `store.ts` builds it via `buildAuthStoreCacheKey({ authPath, options })` which stable-stringifies authPath + config slice + externalCli mode + externalCliProviderIds + externalCliProfileIds + syncExternalCli + allowKeychainPrompt.
- **Why**: prevents cross-caller poisoning while keeping the speedup intact.
- **Test fixup**: `src/secrets/runtime-state.test.ts` updated to seed the cache under the new option-aware key (and documents the option set the cached entry corresponds to).

### 4. Kill switch for the plugin tool-descriptor cache
`src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.ts`

Trivial but useful: setting `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_TOOL_DESCRIPTOR_CACHE=1` makes both the read and write sides no-op. Useful when debugging tool-descriptor recomputation issues (the cache itself is upstream — only the off-knob is new).

## Things the original PR did that this PR does NOT do

- Does **not** re-touch upstream's existing simple per-session `bootstrap-files` cache (`getOrLoadBootstrapFiles`) — upstream's implementation is what we want.
- Does **not** include any plumbing changes that conflicted with upstream's current shape (e.g. the upstream cache module was split into `store-cache.ts`; we adapt to that split rather than reverting it).

## Validation

From a clone of `upstream/main` + this branch:

```
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm exec vitest run \
  src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts \
  src/agents/bootstrap-cache.test.ts \
  src/agents/auth-profiles.store-cache.test.ts \
  src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts \
  src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts \
  src/agents/pi-model-discovery.cache.test.ts
```

Result:

```
 Test Files  10 passed (10)
      Tests  141 passed (141)
```

Smoke tests on directly-touched modules also pass:
- `src/secrets/runtime-state.test.ts` — **1 passed**
- `src/agents/pi-model-discovery.test.ts`, `.auth.test.ts`, `.synthetic-auth.test.ts` — **9 passed**
- `src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth.test.ts` — **17 passed**

No regressions attributable to this PR. Full `pnpm test` was not run because the monorepo's pre-existing test surface is large; targeted tests cover all touched modules and their consumers.

## Predecessor

Closes #76348 (predecessor — same scope, hopelessly stale base; the four still-valuable components above are this PR's payload).

target.title: fix(config): tolerate unknown metadata keys
target.body:
## Summary

OpenClaw currently treats `meta` as a strict object containing only `lastTouchedVersion` and `lastTouchedAt`. If a newer runtime, tool, or config writer adds another bookkeeping field under `meta`, current binaries reject the whole config with an error like:

```text
meta: Unrecognized key: "lastTouchedReason"
```

That can prevent the gateway from starting until the user manually edits `openclaw.json` or repairs the config.

This PR makes only the `meta` object forward-compatible by allowing additional metadata keys while preserving existing strict validation for operational config.

## Why this is intentionally narrow

This is not a general "ignore unknown config keys" change.

Previous broader attempts to tolerate or strip unknown keys at startup were discussed in related PRs such as #47654, #40198, and #65766. There is also an existing fail-closed posture for invalid operational config, e.g. #28205. This PR keeps that posture intact.

The change is limited to `meta`, which is runtime-managed bookkeeping data. Unknown keys under `gateway`, `channels`, providers, auth, plugins, agents, and other operational config still fail validation as before.

## User impact

We observed a real gateway startup failure caused by an extra metadata field:

```json
{
  "meta": {
    "lastTouchedVersion": "2026.5.12",
    "lastTouchedAt": "...",
    "lastTouchedReason": "..."
  }
}
```

The rest of the config was valid. The failure mode was operationally severe because the gateway could not start, but the offending field was metadata rather than executable, routing, provider, auth, or security-relevant configuration.

## What changed

- `meta` now accepts additional unknown metadata keys.
- `meta.lastTouchedVersion` and `meta.lastTouchedAt` remain typed as before.
- The TypeScript config type reflects forward-compatible metadata keys.
- A regression test verifies that unknown metadata keys are accepted and preserved.
- The generated config baseline hash is updated for the intentional schema surface change.

## What did not change

- Strict validation remains in place for the rest of `openclaw.json`.
- Unknown operational config keys still fail validation.
- No automatic config mutation or cleanup is introduced.
- No gateway, auth, provider, channel, or plugin behavior is relaxed.

## Related work

- #80856: related `meta.lastTouched*` handling, but for rejecting user writes to auto-managed metadata paths.
- #68664: broader issue where config validation can block recovery or migration paths.
- #57391: related crash-loop class caused by unrecognized config keys.
- #69631 / #69659: related doctor repair behavior around unknown keys.
- #77802: related doctor repair failure when multiple validation errors exist.
- #72527 / #72535: related version-mismatch config compatibility issue.

## Validation

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/config/config.meta-timestamp-coercion.test.ts` (7 tests)
- `corepack pnpm config:schema:check`
- `corepack pnpm config:docs:check`
- `corepack pnpm config:channels:check`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core --pretty false`
- `corepack pnpm check:no-conflict-markers`
- `git diff --check`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-shims:$PATH corepack pnpm check:changed`

Additional note: while running a broader local config shard, `src/config/io.write-config.test.ts` currently fails one plugin warning assertion. The same failure reproduces on `HEAD~1` in a detached baseline worktree with the same dependencies, so it does not appear to be introduced by this PR.


## Real behavior proof

The proof below uses a temporary redacted `OPENCLAW_HOME` containing only a minimal `openclaw.json`; no real user secrets, channel IDs, tokens, or provider config are included.

### Before this PR: extra metadata key blocks config loading

Command run against `HEAD~1` in a detached baseline worktree:

```text
$ OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp> node --import tsx --input-type=module <loadConfig proof snippet>
Invalid config at <tmp>/.openclaw/openclaw.json:\n- meta: Unrecognized key: "lastTouchedReason"
BEFORE loadConfig failed: - meta: Unrecognized key: "lastTouchedReason"
```

The redacted config used for that proof:

```json
{
  "meta": {
    "lastTouchedVersion": "2026.5.12",
    "lastTouchedAt": "2026-05-17T00:00:00.000Z",
    "lastTouchedReason": "redacted proof note"
  },
  "gateway": { "mode": "local" }
}
```

### After this PR: the same config loads and preserves metadata

Command run against this PR head (`c9b69198`):

```text
$ OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp> node --import tsx --input-type=module <loadConfig proof snippet>
AFTER loadConfig ok: true
AFTER preserved meta.lastTouchedReason: redacted proof note
```

This exercises the real `loadConfig()` path from `src/config/config.ts`, not just a unit-test assertion.

### Operational unknown keys still fail closed

Command run against this PR head (`c9b69198`) with an unknown key under `gateway`:

```text
$ OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp> node --import tsx --input-type=module <loadConfig proof snippet>
Invalid config at <tmp>/.openclaw/openclaw.json:\n- gateway: Unrecognized key: "redactedUnknownKey"
AFTER operational unknown failed: - gateway: Unrecognized key: "redactedUnknownKey"
```

This confirms the change is limited to runtime metadata and does not broadly relax operational config validation.

target.title: Add inbound_observed hook for skipped inbound messages
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a read-only `inbound_observed` plugin hook for passive inbound visibility, then emits it from Telegram when a group message is skipped by `requireMention`/mention gating.

This gives archive/audit/history plugins a supported way to observe messages that are visible to the gateway but intentionally do not enter an agent session or trigger a reply.

## Why

`message_received` currently represents messages that have entered the later dispatch/session path. Moving it earlier would risk changing existing plugin semantics. A dedicated observational hook keeps real-time session routing lean while still enabling full-history archive plugins to record skipped inbound messages.

The new hook is intentionally narrow:

- read-only event payload
- fire-and-forget execution
- no session creation
- no reply/routing override
- includes `skipped` and `skipReason` metadata for mention-gated drops

## Validation

- `pnpm vitest run src/plugins/wired-hooks-message.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts src/hooks/message-hook-mappers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.group-body.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm check:test-types`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Telegram group messages skipped by `requireMention` were invisible to passive archive/history plugins because they returned before the normal dispatch/session hook path. This patch adds a read-only `inbound_observed` hook and emits it for those skipped Telegram group messages.

**Real environment tested:** Live OpenClaw server on the maintainer's local Mac mini, using the local patched source checkout and the existing `conversation-archive` plugin.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
scripts/source-switch-local.sh
scripts/local-status.sh
scripts/check-conversation-archive.sh --hours 24
node --check /Users/cindy/.openclaw/extensions/conversation-archive/index.js
```

I also ran a plugin-level smoke that invoked `conversation-archive` through `inbound_observed` and wrote a raw archive JSONL entry.

**Evidence after fix:**

Representative redacted live output from the patched server:

```text
OpenClaw status
service check
  launchd: system/ai.openclaw.gateway.headless running
  listener: 17185 (port 18789)

Channels
  Telegram ON OK
  BlueBubbles ON OK
  FoloToy ON OK
  @larksuite/openclaw-lark ON OK

Telegram skipped-group inbound_observed archive hook patch is present
workspace archive directory exists and is writable
workspace latest archive sample includes required fields
workspace-caoz latest archive sample includes required fields
workspace-food-group latest archive sample includes required fields
workspace-wife latest archive sample includes required fields

Conversation archive health: OK
```

The plugin-level smoke wrote a JSONL archive entry with:

```text
source: inbound-observed
chat_type: group
text: quiet group message
```

**Observed result after fix:** A skipped Telegram group message can now reach the passive archive plugin via `inbound_observed` without creating an agent session or triggering a reply, and the archive health check still passes on the live setup.

**What was not tested:** I did not send a real test message into a production Telegram group to avoid posting noise. The live proof used the deployed runtime, archive health checks, and a plugin-level smoke for the new hook path.

target.title: fix(status): surface fallback model selections in status mismatch detection
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #96126: `openclaw status` and `session_status` collapse the active (fallback) model into the same `Model:` line as the configured primary, making it impossible to see at a glance whether a session is running its intended model or a fallback.

## What Problem This Solves

Operators running multi-model setups with fallback chains cannot tell whether a session is on its configured primary model or an automatic fallback. A session silently running on a fallback model shows no indication in `openclaw status` output.

## Changes (v2 — ClawSweeper rank-up)

4 files, +56/-11:

- **`status.summary.ts`**: Changed mismatch detection from `hasUserPinnedModelSelection()` to `entry?.modelOverride != null` so auto-fallback selections are also flagged

target.title: fix: force UTF-8 encoding for PowerShell exec output on Windows
target.body:
## Problem

PowerShell defaults to the system console encoding (e.g. Windows-1252 on Spanish/Portuguese systems) when spawned via OpenClaw's exec tool. This causes:
- **Accented characters** (áéíóúñ, ç, ã) render as garbled
- **Non-ASCII text** (CJK, Cyrillic, etc.) corrupted
- **PowerShell stderr** wrapped in noisy ErrorRecord objects

## Root cause

`getShellConfig()` in `shell-utils.ts` constructs:

    powershell.exe -NoProfile -NonInteractive -Command "user command"

Without forcing `[Console]::OutputEncoding = UTF8`, PowerShell uses the system locale encoding (e.g. Windows-1252). `decodeWindowsBufferWithFallback()` already tries UTF-8 first, but the raw output IS NOT UTF-8.

## Fix

Add a PowerShell init snippet as an extra argument after `-Command`:

    [Console]::OutputEncoding=[Text.Encoding]::UTF8;
    [Console]::InputEncoding=[Text.Encoding]::UTF8;
    $OutputEncoding=[Text.Encoding]::UTF8;
    $ErrorActionPreference='SilentlyContinue';

The final argv becomes:

    powershell.exe -NoProfile -NonInteractive -Command "init" "user command"

PowerShell concatenates all remaining args after `-Command`, so the init runs before every user command.

## Files changed

- `src/agents/shell-utils.ts` — Add UTF-8 + error suppression init
- `src/agents/shell-utils.test.ts` — Update test expectations

## Related

- Closes #30640 (and variants for non-Chinese locales)
- Also fixes the PowerShell ErrorRecord noise issue

target.title: fix(memory-wiki): accept title wikilink targets
target.body:
## Summary
- Accept normalized memory-wiki lint targets for page titles and aliases, not only generated paths and basenames.
- Normalize lint link checks for markdown extension, leading `./`, query strings, and heading fragments so Obsidian title links do not report false `broken-wikilink` warnings.
- Add a memory-wiki lint regression test covering title, title-heading, alias, and relative markdown targets.

## Why
Paperclip SUP-1603 found that `openclaw wiki lint` reported broken wikilinks for bridged Obsidian notes even when matching memory-wiki pages existed. The linter only accepted generated slug paths and basenames, while bridge-generated pages preserve the original note title in frontmatter.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw wiki lint` produced false `broken-wikilink` warnings for bridged Obsidian source notes whose links targeted the human-readable page title instead of the generated slug filename.
- **Real environment tested:** Real local OpenClaw memory-wiki vault at `/Users/andrew/.openclaw/wiki/main` on macOS, using this patched source branch against the current vault contents.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran this patched-branch command from the OpenClaw source checkout:

```sh
node --import tsx -e 'import { lintMemoryWikiVault } from "./extensions/memory-wiki/src/lint.ts"; import { resolveMemoryWikiConfig } from "./extensions/memory-wiki/src/config.ts"; const result = await lintMemoryWikiVault(resolveMemoryWikiConfig({ vaultMode: "bridge", vault: { path: "/Users/andrew/.openclaw/wiki/main", renderMode: "obsidian" }, bridge: { enabled: true } })); const broken = result.issues.filter((issue) => issue.code === "broken-wikilink"); console.log(JSON.stringify({ vaultRoot: result.vaultRoot, issueCount: result.issueCount, brokenWikilinks: broken.length, brokenTargets: broken.map((issue) => issue.message), issuesByCode: result.issues.reduce((acc, issue) => { acc[issue.code] = (acc[issue.code] || 0) + 1; return acc; }, {}), reportPath: result.reportPath }, null, 2));'
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real vault after applying this patch:

```json
{
  "vaultRoot": "/Users/andrew/.openclaw/wiki/main",
  "issueCount": 3,
  "brokenWikilinks": 0,
  "brokenTargets": [],
  "issuesByCode": {
    "missing-id": 1,
    "missing-page-type": 1,
    "stale-page": 1
  },
  "reportPath": "/Users/andrew/.openclaw/wiki/main/reports/lint.md"
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The real vault no longer reports any `broken-wikilink` issues. Before this patch, the same real vault reported four title-link `broken-wikilink` warnings for `sources/super-ai-coach-video-script-skill-implementation-plan.md`.
- **What was not tested:** A released npm install containing this patch was not tested because this PR has not been merged or released yet.

## Verification
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/memory-wiki/src/lint.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/memory-wiki/src/lint.ts extensions/memory-wiki/src/lint.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/memory-wiki/src/lint.ts extensions/memory-wiki/src/lint.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`

target.title: fix(auto-reply/followup): surface billing/quota notice instead of silent drop (#80700)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #80700.

When the followup agent's model-fallback chain exhausts on a billing, quota, or rate-limit class rejection from the provider, the catch block in `src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.ts` called `replyOperation.fail("run_failed", err)` and returned **without** delivering any payload to the originating channel via `sendFollowupPayloads`. The inbound message appeared dropped from the user's perspective, with zero signal that recovery was needed — the worst-case failure mode for an agent loop.

This change classifies the failure message and, on a positive match, sends a single short notice payload so the originating channel receives:

> ⚠️ Follow-up reply generation failed (billing/quota/rate-limit). Please retry shortly.

## Approach

Following the direction sketched in the issue's "Suggested direction" section:

1. **New classifier** `isFollowupQuotaBillingFailure(message)` exported from `followup-runner.ts`. Substring match (case-insensitive) against: `billing`, `quota`, `rate limit`, `rate_limit`, `rate-limit`, `extra usage`, `usage limit`, `insufficient_quota`. Empty input returns `false`.
2. **Inside the existing catch block**, after `replyOperation.fail()` and the existing error log, if the classifier matches and `run.sourceReplyDeliveryMode !== "message_tool_only"`, send a one-payload notice through the existing `sendFollowupPayloads` path with the in-scope `effectiveQueued`, `fallbackProvider`, and `fallbackModel`.
3. **Notice-send failures** are caught and logged via `defaultRuntime.error?.(...)` so they cannot mask the original error or throw out of the catch.

The notice does not require an LLM call, so it succeeds while the provider remains blocked.

## Why this shape

- **No model-fallback-layer changes.** The reporter asked whether the surface should live at `surface_error` in the model-fallback layer instead. Keeping the change local to the followup catch block matches the existing `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` suppression pattern already in this file (line 458) and is minimally invasive.
- **`message_tool_only` parity.** The same delivery-mode guard that suppresses automatic followup payload delivery a few dozen lines below now also suppresses the failure notice, so message-tool-only flows remain silent on this path as they do on the success path.
- **Explicit notice failure handling.** Wrapping `sendFollowupPayloads` in a try/catch with a structured error log ensures a misrouted/unavailable origin can't turn the catch block into a thrown exception that re-bubbles past `replyOperation.fail()`.

## Tests

Adds 6 regression tests to `src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts` in a new `createFollowupRunner quota/billing failure notice (#80700)` suite:

1. Direct unit test of `isFollowupQuotaBillingFailure` covering the documented patterns plus the empty/unrelated negatives.
2. Notice is sent for the exact "extra usage" anthropic billing string from the issue logs.
3. Notice is sent for an `insufficient_quota` error.
4. Notice is sent for a `429 ... rate limit` error.
5. Notice is **not** sent for an unrelated `socket hang up` error (negative case).
6. Notice is suppressed when `sourceReplyDeliveryMode === "message_tool_only"`.

Full file suite: **36/36 passing** (30 pre-existing + 6 new). Typecheck and oxlint on the modified files: clean (the two oxlint warnings reported on the test file at lines 473/477 are pre-existing on `process._getActiveHandles`/`_getActiveRequests` and were untouched by this PR).

## Out of scope / open questions for maintainers

- Whether rate-limit vs. billing should surface as visually distinct notices (e.g., "retry shortly" vs. "owner attention needed"). Kept to a single message here.
- Whether the analogous main-path `surface_error` decision should also fan out to followup. Left untouched.
- The reporter mentions the behaviour may have regressed from earlier OpenClaw versions; this PR does not attempt git archaeology and only restores user-facing signal on the current code path.

Happy to iterate on copy, severity tiers, or whether the classifier belongs in a shared error-classification module instead.

target.title: SUP-1563 restore channel responsiveness health
target.body:
## Summary
- Surface real busy/active-run/stale activity state in channel status instead of relying only on connected transport state.
- Record Telegram outbound activity with the correct account id and avoid treating bot-authored Discord messages as human inbound activity.
- Return visible Discord/Telegram acknowledgements when a message is queued or steered behind active channel work, so connected transports do not look silent during busy turns.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord/Telegram transports could be connected while Jarvis appeared silent because a human Discord message was accepted behind active channel work. The fix exposes busy/stale channel work state, prevents false Discord inbound timestamps from bot-authored gateway events, and keeps Telegram accounts connected while Discord proof is checked against the latest human message.
- Real environment tested: Andrew's Mac mini OpenClaw install, gateway on `127.0.0.1:18789`, installed OpenClaw 2026.5.3 runtime plus the SUP-1563 live hotfix, Discord bot account `default`, Telegram accounts `chad`, `default`, `michael`, and `steve`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/openclaw-discord-proof-check.js --json --allow-missing`
  - `curl -fsS --max-time 8 http://127.0.0.1:18789/readyz`
  - `sqlite3 -header -column /Users/andrew/.openclaw/tasks/runs.sqlite "select task_id, source_id, owner_key, runtime, status, datetime(created_at/1000,'unixepoch','localtime') as created, datetime(last_event_at/1000,'unixepoch','localtime') as last_event, substr(error,1,120) as error from task_runs where status in ('running','queued') order by created_at desc limit 20;"`
